axes.description: How far the AI agent acts vs how strictly it presents/asks. Autonomy makes the loop fast; human control keeps invisibility from being AI-created.
Heavy formal machinery (Z3 / Quint / mutation testing) catches more contradictions but slows the loop. Calibration rule: weight of apparatus ∝ cost of an unnoticed conflict.
What stays in the minimal framework core vs what becomes an opt-in pluggable aspect. Core costs every domain; aspects cost only those who load them.
Machine-checkable predicate (deterministic, narrow) vs EARS / free-prose claim (broad, ambiguous). Most claims are prose; the critical core is formalized.
Content-free shipping (zero business data in src/hotam_spec) vs out-of-the-box utility for a fresh adopter. Purity is honest; helpfulness lowers adoption cost.

Relieve operator context pressure by delegating/splitting the domain (horizontal) vs by crystallizing knowledge into the substrate (vertical). Splitting is for irreducible size; crystallizing is for un-offloaded knowledge.

Crystallize knowledge into the free substrate (graph + invariants + generated docs) vs hold it in expensive working context. Substrate knowledge is enforced/regenerable/addressable, so it does not count against an operator's context budget.

Coupled work (dependency edges between requirements/operators/entities) must be processed sequentially; independent sub-graphs can be delegated to parallel sub-operators. The dependency-graph topology — not a guess — decides which, and domains are split along lines of independence.
Conflating the methodology's own concepts with the modeled domain's (Task-vs-Action; Conflict-as-methodology-node vs Conflict-as-business-event). Two altitudes must stay separable.

stakeholders.name: AI agent
stakeholders.domain: the three roles (Detector / Socratic / Historian) and the hard boundary

stakeholders.name: Domain user
stakeholders.domain: a practitioner populating their business domain under spec/content/

stakeholders.name: Framework reviewer
stakeholders.domain: independent stewardship of tensions between author and AI

assumptions.statement: The dependency arrow between agent code and the framework body runs one way only: an agent's code may import hotam_spec.* as shared infrastructure, but hotam_spec.* (and spec/tools/*.py) must never import back from any agent's private tools/ directory. — [HOLDS] Re-stated 2026-07-12 for the Go port: the 'framework body' the original statement called hotam_spec.* is now the Go framework packages (internal/* and cmd/hotam), and the 'shared tools' it called spec/tools/*.py are not yet ported. The directional principle is unchanged -- an agent's code may import the framework body, and the framework body must never import back from any agent's private tools/ directory. The assumption HOLDS (no agent has been scaffolded in the Go port, so the arrow is vacuously one-way); only the package identifiers were Python-isms, now corrected to the Go packages.

The framework can model its own design coherently — Hotam-Spec's own methodology fits its own ontology with no special-casing. — [IMPLEMENTS] Steward verdict 2026-07-03 (verbatim): 'нужно еще один статус - IMPLEMENTS - значит, что мы пытаемся это сделать, мы стремимся к этому, хотим этого'. A-bootstrap-self-applies is not a fact under question but an aspiration we strive toward — re-typed from UNCERTAIN (58 dependents, top pulse line) to the volitional IMPLEMENTS род rather than re-affirmed HOLDS. Changes род and drops the P4 doubt signal honestly.
Knowledge living only in working context is lost on context auto-compaction; only the crystallized substrate survives.

A contradiction can only carry its shared knowledge (axis, context, shared root) if it is modeled as a first-class NODE; a `conflicts_with` edge between requirements holds nothing and cannot be clustered or stewarded.
An empty spec/content/ is a legitimate ship state — the framework's value is real even before any domain is populated.

The framework body already splits, purely in code, into a core stratum (the typed node classes plus the graph/proposal/invariant layers built directly on them, including scope_projection which is consumed inside a core check) and a periphery stratum built OVER a fully-formed graph (attention, reflection, invariants_table_engine); the dependency arrow runs one way only -- periphery imports core, core never imports periphery.
Operators are finite-context agents; an operator's problem domain must fit its context.

The framework body (hotam_spec.*) is shared infrastructure any agent's code may import; it is owned by no single agent -- ownership is a governance convention, not a per-agent claim. — [HOLDS] Re-stated 2026-07-12 for the Go port: the 'framework body' the original statement called hotam_spec.* is now the Go framework packages (internal/* and cmd/hotam). It is shared infrastructure any agent's code may import and is owned by no single agent -- ownership is a governance convention, not a per-agent claim. The assumption HOLDS unchanged in essence; only the package identifier was a Python-ism, now corrected to the Go packages.
The whole tension graph fits in one Python process; streaming/persistence is not required.

Most knowledge can be expressed as a node; where it cannot, that resistance is itself a signal of a missing ontology type. — [IMPLEMENTS] Steward verdict 2026-07-03 (V3), variant B verbatim: «Б - мы к этому стремимся, при нахождении несоответствия пытаемся исправить на месте сами или через агента-руку». This is not a fact-claim about the world but a VOLITIONAL striving (R-assumption-implements-state): most knowledge is not KNOWN to be crystallizable, but the operator STRIVES to crystallize it and, on finding a mismatch, repairs it in place itself or via a hand-agent. Re-typing UNCERTAIN → IMPLEMENTS retires the standing P4 doubt signal and records the aspiration.

The Hotam-Spec ontology (Requirement/Conflict/Assumption/Axis/EntityType/Operator/Goal/Process, plus the check_* invariant layer and the enforcement gradient PROSE->STRUCTURAL->ENFORCED) transfers to a real external, non-meta business domain (a first-time consumer populating an empty graph.py under domains/<name>/ for their own product/process) without needing to reshape the ontology itself. — [UNCERTAIN] Re-stated 2026-07-12 for the Go port: the original statement's 'empty graph.py under domains/<name>/' is now 'empty graph.json under domains/<name>/' (the Go CLI's graph is plain JSON data at domains/<name>/graph.json, written only via hotam apply-proposal). The ontology-transfer premise is unchanged -- the Requirement/Conflict/Assumption/Axis/EntityType/Operator/Goal/Process types, the check_* invariant layer, and the PROSE->STRUCTURAL->ENFORCED gradient transfer to a real external business domain without reshaping the ontology. Still UNCERTAIN: unproven by a first external Go consumer.

For the bulk of requirements, EARS-style prose claims plus structural invariants suffice; formal predicates are reserved for the critical core. — [DEAD] Steward verdict 2026-07-03 (V2), verbatim: «кажется, теста никогда не достаточно. У всего должны быть модели, модели должны иметь простые методы (которые легко читаются (пару строчек), и текстовое поисание, по которому легко проверить, что имплементация метода соотвествует описанию. И должны быть тесты, которые вызывают эти методы. Еще должны быть стейты ... это мы в самом начале собуждали. Тексты сами по себе - это воздух без зелми». Prose alone never sufficed: a claim is trustworthy only when grounded in a model (short readable methods + description checkable against implementation + tests invoking them + states). REPLACED by the HOLDS assumption A-text-grounded-in-models; all 37 dependent requirements were re-linked onto it before this kill.

The framework runs on Python 3.12+ with uv + ruff + pytest + hypothesis. — [HOLDS] Re-stated 2026-07-12 for the Go port: the framework now runs on Go (go.mod module github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpecGo, the internal/* packages, the cmd/hotam CLI, verified by `go test ./...`), NOT the Python 3.12 + uv + ruff + pytest + hypothesis stack described in the original statement. The earlier Python stack described the pre-port prototype and is superseded. The essence still HOLDS -- the framework rests on a concrete, toolable implementation stack -- only the language changed (Python -> Go). The ~80 requirements resting on this assumption depend on the framework being concretely implemented, which remains true; killing it to DEAD would misrepresent that, so it is re-affirmed HOLDS with the language correction recorded here. (The stale machine_check 'python.version >= (3, 12)' is retained only because an AssumptionTransition cannot rewrite it; it is no longer the live stack check.)

In the current phase all stakeholder roles (domain-user/framework-reviewer/framework-author in self, dev-steward/pipeline-operator in hotam-dev) are worn by one human; role-separation checks guard structure, not real independence
At least two distinct human stakeholders exist who are willing to steward conflicts.

Text alone is air without ground: every claim is grounded in a model that has short readable methods, a textual description checkable against the method's implementation, tests that invoke those methods, and states (lifecycles). A requirement resting on this assumption is trustworthy only insofar as such a grounding model exists behind it.

requirements.claim: A CLI command (`hotam apply-proposal`, `cmd/hotam`) shall consume an approved Proposed* JSON proposal and mechanically apply the change to the domain's graph.json (`domains/<name>/graph.json`) via `internal/proposal.Apply`, never by a hand-edit.
requirements.why: Atom of R-active-loop-playbooks (tool concern). `hotam apply-proposal` (and the `hotam land` pipeline that wraps apply + gen-spec + all-violations) lands a steward-approved JSON proposal into domains/<name>/graph.json and runs the regen+verify pipeline (internal/proposal + internal/generator + internal/invariants). The graph is plain JSON data (not source code spliced by AST), so applying a proposal is a JSON write, never an AST edit (previously implemented as tools/apply_proposal.py + spec/content/graph.py). settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: A set of Proposed* struct kinds (ProposedRequirement, ProposedConflictTransition, ProposedRejection, ProposedConflict, ProposedAssumptionTransition, et al.) shall exist in `internal/proposal` as the typed protocol for steward-approved operator changes, decoded strictly (snake_case json tags, unknown fields rejected).
requirements.why: Atom of R-active-loop-playbooks (data-model concern). `internal/proposal/types.go` defines the Proposed* structs (12 kinds today: Requirement, ConflictTransition, Rejection, Conflict, OperatorBudget, Axis, Stakeholder, Assumption, AssumptionTransition, ConflictMemberUpdate, EntityType, ReviewMark). Each struct carries its own validate() (validate.go) and mutate() (mutate.go) that apply it to the graph; the original three-kind protocol grew as the graph gained first-class types.

requirements.claim: Agent conduct -- the highest abstraction -- shall not be faked as code-checked: what code can verify is ENFORCED with a real test, what only conscience can verify is an honest PROSE rule, and the two are kept explicitly separate.
requirements.why: Вердикт стьюарда (2026-07-05, подписан): «мы не можем проверить кодом работу агента — это высшая абстракция кодом не проверяется. Поэтому это нужно оформлять как правило, если оно нам действительно нужно и полезно. И так наверное многие вещи. Мы даже протестировать это не сможем. И кажется нужно отделять то, что мы можем протестировать от просто правил. При чем правила желательно формулировать так, чтобы быстро показывались противоречия при их внесении с изменениями.» Два мёртвых метра подтвердили вердикт: boot-cite Stop-хук писал 10-13% в журнал, который ничто не читало (form-metric theatre); spawn-log был пуст при десятках спавнов. Принцип даёт им судьбу: boot-cite-measured REJECTED как фальшивый ENFORCED, а spawn-log атом честно маркирован STRUCTURAL. Уточняет R-enforcement-first-class (механика полей) и R-enforceability-kind-declared (ENFORCEABLE vs INHERENTLY_PROSE): те задают КАК маркировать, этот принцип задаёт ЗАЧЕМ и КОГДА какой уровень выбирать.

requirements.claim: Every agent at domains/<name>/agents/<a>/ (including recursively-nested sub-agents) shall contain a docs/ subdirectory for the agent's private notes, separate from any generated content.
requirements.why: Agents accumulate private reasoning -- checkpoints, open questions, steward notes -- that must not mix with generated content or the parent operator crystal. A dedicated docs/ directory provides a stable, predictable location that survives crystal regeneration. Enforcing check check_agent_has_docs_subdir is registered in internal/invariants but is an HONEST NO-OP: it checks the FILE SYSTEM STRUCTURE of agent directories on disk, which the in-memory-graph invariant contract has no access to (filesystem-coherence checks belong to a future agent-scaffolding layer), and no agent has been scaffolded yet. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: An Assumption's status field shall admit a fourth value IMPLEMENTS denoting a VOLITIONAL aspiration (a claim we strive to make true), categorically distinct from the three epistemic fact-claim statuses HOLDS/UNCERTAIN/DEAD.
requirements.why: Steward verdict (2026-07-03, verbatim): 'нужно еще один статус - IMPLEMENTS - значит, что мы пытаемся это сделать, мы стремимся к этому, хотим этого'. HOLDS/UNCERTAIN/DEAD are epistemic (they answer 'is this true?'); IMPLEMENTS is volitional (it answers 'do we want this to become true, and are we working toward it?'). Three consequences follow from its non-epistemic nature and are guaranteed by the status-keyed predicates in internal/diagnose that use exact equality: (a) the UNCERTAIN-aging predicate does NOT touch it (an aspiration is not an unresolved doubt, so no P4 review-debt); (b) it is NEVER dead-assumption fallout (an aspiration is not a broken premise); (c) its transitions and signoff (full table in ProposedAssumptionTransition, internal/proposal): IMPLEMENTS->HOLDS, IMPLEMENTS->DEAD, UNCERTAIN->IMPLEMENTS and HOLDS->IMPLEMENTS all require a human decided_by; entry without signoff remains UNCERTAIN-only. IMPLEMENTS is a member of internal/ontology.AssumptionStates and is enforced first-class by check_assumption_status_valid (internal/invariants, registered in all_violations.go); ProposedAssumptionTransition.validate adds IMPLEMENTS to the decided_by-required set (DEAD, HOLDS, IMPLEMENTS).

requirements.claim: The proposal protocol shall include a ProposedAssumptionTransition kind (kind='AssumptionTransition') that changes an existing Assumption's status (HOLDS/UNCERTAIN/DEAD/IMPLEMENTS) in place via `hotam apply-proposal`, appending the reason to its statement and never deleting the node.
requirements.why: Assumption drift is the declared root of the methodology (§Assumption -- 'the root of context drift'), yet without this atom the graph had no kill-path: ProposedAssumption was add-only and the hand-edit lock blocked any status change, so a DEAD assumption's cluster-wide fallout could never fire. ProposedAssumptionTransition.mutate (internal/proposal/mutate.go) UPDATES the existing Assumption's status and APPENDS '— [STATUS] reason' to its statement so the falsification trail survives (mirror of R-rejected-preserved-not-deleted), driven through `hotam apply-proposal` (cmd/hotam). Signoff asymmetry (decided honestly against §Assumption, thinking/assumption.md): DEAD (kills a premise, cluster-wide fallout) and HOLDS (re-affirms, silences the review signal) both REQUIRE a decided_by Stakeholder -- the same altitude as closing a Conflict (R-decided-needs-human-signoff, R-ai-presents-not-decides); UNCERTAIN merely RAISES a question (adds a P4 review signal, removes none), which is exactly the operator's PRESENT step, so the agent may present it alone. The transition fails closed to the full suite because an assumption carries no enforced_by to bound its fallout.

requirements.claim: An agent-agnostic attention-source registry shall exist whose collect() runs every registered source and returns typed AttentionSignal records, with the graph-source diagnosis deterministic across runs.
requirements.why: Steward verdict (2026-07-03): the 'pay attention here' signals were scattered -- half in the what-now diagnosis, half as CLI-only bands -- with no single, agent-agnostic, importable-as-a-library place an arbitrary agent could call to learn what needs attention. This lifts them into ONE core with an explicit registry of named sources, each producing typed AttentionSignal records the substrate and any agent share; the graph-source diagnosis is deterministic (byte-identical across runs) so it can feed the byte-stable substrate. requirements.claim: The live attention list collect(g) shall be a superset of the deterministic graph subset diagnose(g), equal to it exactly when no runtime-fs sources are injected.
requirements.why: The honest split at the centre of the attention design: the SUBSTRATE (gen-spec / LIVE-STATE) must stay byte-stable (R-deterministic-generation), so it consumes ONLY the deterministic graph diagnosis; the LIVE AGENT wants more -- pending proposals, open tickets, stale audit, unread revisit markers -- which read the filesystem and are non-deterministic. Rather than couple those into the graph diagnosis (breaking determinism and polluting graph tests), the filesystem sources are INJECTED by the live consumer via collect(runtime_sources=...): the registry holds graph-only sources, the live consumer supplies the fs half, so collect(agent) is a superset of diagnose(substrate). Not yet implemented: the attention layer is Planned (the deterministic graph diagnosis it builds on does live in internal/diagnose, Implemented); previously implemented in src/hotam_spec/attention.py with what_now.runtime_fs_sources(), guarded by tests/test_attention_core.py. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Authored spec/ coverage of a domain SHALL proceed in layer order, general before specific, the same skeleton-first discipline R-domain-founded-in-wave-order already applies to graph founding: (1) MODELS -- first author the full skeleton of the domain's object model, every aggregate/entity/value-object that exists, before any one of them is fleshed out; (2) FIELDS -- then author each model's fields/attributes; (3) METHODS -- then author operations/methods, ALL the interactions between models (behavior, invariants, error paths), not just one model's; (4) TESTS -- then author the executable tests that prove requirements against that code. An agent building authored spec/ shall NOT take one requirement all the way from model to test before starting the next; it shall complete each layer across the WHOLE domain before advancing to the next layer.
requirements.why: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §5 ("Гейт готовности", steward decision 2026-07-16) states the layer order explicitly: "Сначала полная картина моделей, потом деталь -- не 'одно требование целиком до конца, потом следующее'", and names the four layers (models -> fields -> methods -> tests) as the coverage progression a coverage-audit tool tracks ("Coverage-дисциплина отслеживает, на каком слое домен сейчас"). This is the code-authoring analogue of R-domain-founded-in-wave-order (#203), which already establishes the identical general-before-specific principle for GRAPH founding (skeleton -> artifacts -> rules -> tensions -> data) -- this requirement is the sibling discipline for CODE authoring inside a founded domain's spec/ tree, explicitly refining it rather than duplicating it: R-domain-founded-in-wave-order governs the order in which graph.json nodes (manifest, Process, EntityType, Requirement, Assumption/Conflict, EntityInstance) are landed; this requirement governs the order in which authored Go code (spec/model, spec/model fields, spec/application+policy methods, spec/tests) is written once a domain's requirements already exist. Both are honest roadmap-debt PROSE claims today: no coverage-audit tool yet exists that mechanically detects an agent skipping ahead (e.g. writing one requirement's test before another requirement's model exists) -- PLAN §5's promised "Coverage-дисциплина" and PLAN §7's COVERAGE.md generated projection are the not-yet-built enforcers (tracked by PLAN §11 task #225), so this requirement is honest operator discipline today, exactly the same shape as R-domain-founded-in-wave-order's own honest-PROSE status.
evidence: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §5 states the four-layer order verbatim and names the not-yet-built coverage-audit tool as the future enforcer

requirements.claim: Every requirement's implemented_by/verified_by entries (path-qualified file:symbol / file:test references into a domain's authored spec/ tree) shall be mechanically checked: the named symbol/test shall exist; a verified_by test shall be non-vacuous (a real assertion or exercising branch, not t.Log-only or empty) and free of an unconditional top-level t.Skip; the same verified_by entry shall not be formally cited by two or more requirements that are not all mutually related via Relations; and a resolvable, non-vacuous, non-skipped verified_by test shall actually PASS when compiled and executed via `go test`, not merely be shaped like a proof.
requirements.why: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §6 (the mechanical-checks catalog) requires a STRUCTURAL floor under the authored-spec era's implemented_by/verified_by fields (added additively in a prior session, internal/ontology/requirement.go): a stale, vacuous, skipped, promiscuously-reused, or NEVER-ACTUALLY-RUN reference would let ENFORCED mean nothing for a domain's own authored code+tests, exactly the failure PLAN §1 documents from the old generator era ("t.Log('no structural atom') in 10 of 22 requirements"). Seven atomic check_* invariants implement this (internal/invariants/authored_links.go): check_implemented_by_symbol_resolvable (the symbol -- function, method, or type -- really exists), check_verified_by_test_resolvable (the test really exists and is Test*-shaped with the real go-test signature), check_verified_by_test_has_teeth (a resolvable verified_by test whose body is empty or t.Log-only fires), check_verified_by_test_no_skip (an unconditional top-level t.Skip/t.Skipf fires), check_verified_by_no_unrelated_reuse (the same verified_by entry formally cited by requirements that do not all collapse into one connected component under the Relations adjacency is a red flag), and check_verified_by_test_passes (@fh finding F1, Probe C, added this session: every check before it is AST-only -- it inspects the TEST's source text but never actually COMPILES or RUNS it, so a requirement could stay ENFORCED with a verified_by test that fails or does not even compile as long as the test's SHAPE looked right; Probe C demonstrated this concretely by gutting an implementation, e.g. a validation method rewritten to unconditionally return nil, which turned `go test` red for that package while every AST-only check stayed green. check_verified_by_test_passes closes that gap: for every verified_by entry that already resolved, has teeth, and is not skipped, it actually invokes `go test -run` via internal/gate.RunVerifiedByTest against the owning Go module -- the domain's own spec/ module for an ordinary domain, or the engine's own module for a self-hosting domain, resolved by gate.ModuleRoot walking up to the nearest go.mod -- and requires PASS; a non-zero exit, a FAIL in the output, or a compile failure fires a violation naming the test and a bounded tail of the actual go-test output. PERFORMANCE (design decision A): results are memoized in a process-lifetime cache (gate.RunVerifiedByTest) keyed by (package directory, test name) with content-hash invalidation over go.mod/go.sum plus every *.go file in the test's own package directory (gate.hashPackageInputs) -- not just the two named files, since `go test` compiles the WHOLE package and Probe C's mutation shape is exactly an impl-file edit in a file the test does not itself name -- so an unchanged spec is not re-run on every all-violations call, while any relevant edit forces a real re-run. RECURSION: a self-hosting domain's own graph names its own engine test files as verified_by targets, and several of the engine's own package test suites (internal/invariants, internal/generator, cmd/hotam) themselves call AllViolations against that same real graph -- gate.RunVerifiedByTest carries a recursion guard (HOTAM_VERIFIED_BY_EXEC_GUARD env var set on every spawned `go test` child) so a nested invocation reports Skipped (no violation either way) instead of spawning an unbounded further generation; the outer, non-nested invocation is the one that actually proves it. CONCURRENCY: checkVerifiedByTestPasses fans independent RunVerifiedByTest calls out across a small bounded worker pool (4 workers) rather than running them sequentially, since each is I/O-bound subprocess work, not CPU-bound.) HONESTY BOUNDARY (§6): these checks hold the structural floor only -- symbol/test exists, runs, is not hollow, is not skipped, is not promiscuously reused, and (as of check_verified_by_test_passes) actually PASSES -- they do NOT and cannot verify that a test SEMANTICALLY proves the requirement's claim; that remains a mirror audit performed by a human or an LLM reading the pair (requirement text, authored code+test), never encoded as a lexical/semantic heuristic in the graph invariant layer. All seven checks are structurally NO-OP for any requirement with empty implemented_by/verified_by (both fields remain optional); mutation-proof coverage lives in internal/gate/spec_resolver_test.go, internal/gate/test_exec_test.go, and internal/invariants/authored_links_test.go (each check has a break-then-fix round-trip test proving it fires on a real mutated fixture and clears once fixed; check_verified_by_test_passes additionally has TestRunVerifiedByTest_MUTATION_CacheInvalidatesOnImplChange, an impl-only mutation round-trip proving the cache is invalidated by a sibling file edit, never just a test-file edit).
evidence: cd D:/ai_dev/prat/HotamSpec && go test ./internal/gate/... ./internal/invariants/... -run 'RunVerifiedByTest|CheckVerifiedByTestPasses|CheckImplementedBySymbolResolvable_MUTATION' -v : PASS (F1 remediation, 2026-07-16); check_verified_by_test_passes (internal/invariants/authored_links.go) is the 7th check_* invariant this requirement's claim/why now names, closing @fh finding F1 (Probe C: AST-only checks stayed green against a gutted implementation because none of them ever ran the test); TestRunVerifiedByTest_MUTATION_CacheInvalidatesOnImplChange (internal/gate/test_exec_test.go) is the mutation round-trip proving RunVerifiedByTest actually executes the test and that the content-hash cache invalidates on an impl-file-only edit; enforced_by extended with check_verified_by_test_passes alongside the five pre-existing checks -- all six were already SETTLED/ENFORCED via this same requirement, this UPDATE only adds the 7th

requirements.claim: Every generated document that describes a domain's authored spec/ tree -- TRACEABILITY.md (requirement -> implemented_by symbol -> verified_by test), MODELS.md (an inventory of authored model structs/fields/methods/invariants extracted from spec/model via go/ast), COVERAGE.md (which SETTLED+ENFORCEABLE requirements have real implemented_by/verified_by carriers versus roadmap debt, and which layer per R-authored-spec-layer-progression the domain currently sits at), and UNENFORCED.md's authored-era burn-down -- shall be a GENERATED projection assembled by `hotam gen-spec` from the graph plus the spec/ resolver (internal/gate/spec_resolver.go), and shall NEVER be hand-written or hand-edited. spec/ itself (the domain's authored model/application/policy/tests code) is the one artifact in this pipeline authored by hand and NEVER regenerated or overwritten by the engine; docs/gen/ is the generated side and is ALWAYS safe to regenerate and overwrite.
requirements.why: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §3 draws the authored/generated line at the directory level ("spec/ -- AUTHORED код-спека -- пишется РУКАМИ, движок НЕ перезаписывает" versus "docs/gen/ -- ГЕНЕРИРУЕМЫЕ проекции -- движок перезаписывает"), and §7 ("Генерируемые проекции (вместо кода)") names the specific four documents this requirement covers: TRACEABILITY.md, MODELS.md, COVERAGE.md, UNENFORCED.md. This is the natural-language DEFINITION half of the authored/generated split; it is distinct from R-domain-overview-projection (#201/PIPELINE.md), which governs a DIFFERENT generated document (the Process/EntityType-driven whole-domain overview, unrelated to spec/ code) and from R-requirement-freshness-fields (#the history mechanism), which governs a DIFFERENT derived artifact (per-node History, not a whole document). Naming this requirement closes the same gap R-domain-overview-projection closes for PIPELINE.md, but for the NEW authored-spec-era documents PLAN §7 introduces -- without this node, an agent reading the self-domain graph would find no anchor stating that TRACEABILITY/MODELS/COVERAGE/UNENFORCED are derived, not authored, and might be tempted to hand-edit them the way spec/ itself is meant to be hand-edited, exactly inverting PLAN §3's authored/generated boundary. Honestly PROSE/feature-blocked today (R-speculative-aspects-frozen's pattern): none of these four generators exist yet in internal/generator (task #225, PLAN §11, not yet executed at authorship time) -- this requirement fixes the CONTRACT the future generator must honor (never hand-write these four documents) before the generator itself is built, the same order R-domain-overview-projection followed for PIPELINE.md.

requirements.claim: The framework names no target backends: the core (graph/JSON proposals/CLI/Go test suite) stays backend-neutral by construction, and adapting the skin is the adopting agent's own concern.
requirements.why: M37. Steward verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «не важно -- каждый умный агент под себя допишет» (English: 'it doesn't matter -- every smart agent will adapt it for itself'). This settles M37 by declining to name concrete backends (CI runner / alternate coding agent / programmatic or human steward): the core surface (cmd/hotam CLIs, JSON Proposed* shapes, go test verification) already has no dependency on any one agent's runtime, so it stays backend-neutral by construction rather than by a designed OperatorBackend protocol. R-operator-backend-protocol remains gated/unbuilt -- the steward's answer is that building it now would be speculative engineering against hypothetical backends R-speculative-aspects-frozen already warns against, not that the protocol is wrong in principle. REQUALIFIED 2026-07-02 (Wave 7 move 2 honesty pass): previously carried default enforceability=ENFORCEABLE, which listed it as closeable debt in UNENFORCED.md -- but no check_* can ever verify 'no target backend is named' or 'adapting the skin is the adopting agent's own concern' as a runtime property of the committed graph (it is a design-stance/non-decision about scope, not a structural fact). This is the same honesty class as R-initiator-supplies-domain-content and R-observation-evidence-scope: a real, permanent positional discipline that is INHERENTLY_PROSE, not ENFORCEABLE-but-unbuilt. The measurable SLICE of this claim already reached ENFORCED separately: R-core-imports-stdlib-or-hotam-spec-only mechanically verifies the CONSTRUCTION half -- go.mod proving the module imports nothing beyond the Go standard library -- so no backend dependency has silently crept into the core; R-backend-scope itself states the broader naming/positional stance, which stays INHERENTLY_PROSE.

requirements.claim: The three-level enforcement gradient (PROSE/STRUCTURAL/ENFORCED) shall be collapsed into a binary ENFORCED/NOT_ENFORCED distinction plus a separate inherently_prose flag, because the middle STRUCTURAL tier is redundant.
requirements.why: REJECTED — REPLACES by enforceability (ENFORCEABLE | INHERENTLY_PROSE) + Requirement.is_closeable_debt(). The ratchet pressure the binarization was meant to deliver is ALREADY binary and lives on a different axis than the descriptive enforcement gradient. is_closeable_debt() returns `enforcement != ENFORCED and enforceability == ENFORCEABLE` — it ignores the PROSE-vs-STRUCTURAL distinction entirely and keys the only normative pressure (real, closeable debt) off enforceability, not off which of the three descriptive tiers a claim sits in. Concretely: most STRUCTURAL nodes already carry enforceability=INHERENTLY_PROSE and are therefore NOT counted as debt; after Этап T (#122) real closeable debt is down to 2 nodes. Binarizing PROSE/STRUCTURAL/ENFORCED would be a ~50-site code refactor (proposal.py, apply_proposal.py, gen_spec.py, PROPOSAL-REFERENCE.md, ~270 existing graph nodes) for exactly zero change to the debt metric. The three-level enforcement value is retained as a DESCRIPTIVE, non-normative scale (how deeply a claim is crystallized: recorded / addressable / auto-checked) — useful for reading and reporting — while all normative convergence pressure stays on the already-binary enforceability + is_closeable_debt() pair. REVISIT WHEN a second, independent normative consumer of the STRUCTURAL tier appears (something that must treat STRUCTURAL differently from PROSE for a gate/metric, not merely for display). — (was: external-review backlog item D3 — binarize the enforcement gradient.) — (was: External-review backlog item D3. Proposed to binarize the enforcement scale.)

requirements.claim: An operator's context budget shall measure the SIZE of its resident business content in bytes/chars (tokens when the host exposes them), not node counts.
requirements.why: M17. Steward verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «наверное, это размер бизнес-требований. Лучше в байтах или в токенах» (English: 'probably it's the size of the business requirements. Better in bytes or in tokens'). Landed reality already matches: BudgetMeasureCRYSTAL_CHARS ('measure = len(root CLAUDE.md) in characters') in internal/ontology/operator.go is the current byte-approximation of resident business content, wired into check_operator_within_budget (internal/invariants/lifecycle_checks.go) and used by the live meta-domain operator. BudgetMeasureTOKEN_ESTIMATE is declared in the BudgetMeasures map (internal/ontology/operator.go) as the token-exact seam the steward names ('в токенах') for when the host exposes token counts, but is deferred (not measured yet) since no host API is wired. BudgetMeasureNODE_COUNT (the original M17 default, size = len(requirements)+len(conflicts)+len(assumptions)) is DEMOTED to a legacy measure: it counts the crystallized SUBSTRATE, which R-working-vs-substrate-budget declares free, so it over-penalizes crystallizing; it remains selectable only for operators/tests that opted into it before CRYSTAL_CHARS existed.

requirements.claim: CLAUDE.md (the director's crystal) shall not exceed 1_000_000 / φ ≈ 618033 tokens; on approach, the operator crystallizes/splits rather than letting the crystal swell.
requirements.why: REJECTED -- REPLACES by R-budget-measure + R-working-vs-substrate-budget (both SETTLED/ENFORCED) via CRYSTAL_CHARS. The phi-cap claim hardcoded a numeric ceiling (1_000_000 / phi ~= 618033 tokens) picked before the operator had any real measure of its own crystal size, and it named TOKENS while the operator's actual context arithmetic (check_operator_within_budget) counts CHARS against a host cap -- a unit mismatch that would have silently lied the moment anyone tried to wire a check against it. R-budget-measure already settles the honest unit (bytes/chars, not node counts, not a borrowed-constant token cap) and R-working-vs-substrate-budget already settles what the budget bounds (the WORKING store, leaving crystallized substrate free). check_operator_within_budget's CRYSTAL_CHARS measure (root CLAUDE.md char-length vs a declared host cap, currently 150000) is the real, live, already-ENFORCED mechanism; the phi-cap was a speculative alternate arithmetic that never had an enforcer and would have required reconciling two different budget units had it ever been built. R-claude-md-tree-of-crystals's trigger is retargeted from this dead cap onto the CRYSTAL_CHARS warn threshold in a separate proposal. — (was: The context-bounded-delegation law (R-context-bounded-delegation) applied to the operator's OWN body, not just the graph. BUILD-TRIGGER: CLAUDE.md crosses ~50% of the φ-cap (~309K tokens) — today it is ~7K (~1%), so a budget CHECK now would be machinery guarding a condition that cannot fire. The LIVE-STATE block already reports φ-headroom; the check + the REFLECTION P0 wiring land when headroom actually narrows.)

requirements.claim: While a repository has exactly one domain and zero actively-spawned concurrent sub-agents, `hotam gen-spec` shall generate exactly one CLAUDE.md file at repository root containing all operator-prompt content.

requirements.claim: CLAUDE.md shall be generated by substituting <!-- mind --> and <!-- business --> placeholders in CLAUDE.md.template.txt with rendered content, preserving all other template text verbatim.
requirements.why: The prior sentinel-surgery approach (editing ~10 separate BEGIN/END marker pairs scattered through an existing file) made it impossible for a human to add durable plain-text notes anywhere in CLAUDE.md without risking accidental clobbering by a future block-insertion bug. The template model inverts this: exactly two named placeholders are substituted; everything else in the template -- including any hand-written notes a human adds -- survives every regeneration verbatim. Simpler mental model, same anti-drift guarantee for the two generated zones. (Wave 1 seed-coherence pass: enforced_by's second entry 'test_regen_byte_identical' is ambiguous -- two test files each define a function with that bare name -- corrected to the specific file, caught by the new check_enforced_by_resolvable invariant.) requirements.claim: Committed framework Go source (internal/*, cmd/hotam/*) shall never reference the host home directory co-located with a filesystem-write sink -- the framework writes only within its launch directory, never into the host home.
requirements.why: SPLIT from R-work-within-launch-dir 2026-07-13 (task #115, decision brief docs/reviews/2026-07-13-category-c2-decision-brief.md section 6 option (a')): the original 'operator shall confine all file mutations to its launch directory' claim bundled two genuinely different things -- (1) committed framework CODE that writes into the host home (statically, mechanically checkable: an AST scanner over committed source), and (2) a live agent shelling out at RUNTIME through bash/tool-calls to touch the home dir (not statically checkable -- discipline of prose). This atom is the structural/mechanically-checkable half (1); the residual live-conduct half (2) stays at R-work-within-launch-dir (now INHERENTLY_PROSE, same category as R-agent-conduct-is-rules-not-tests). The original enforcement was a Python AST scanner (tests/test_launch_dir_write_scope.py, ENFORCED 2026-07-10) that was NOT carried forward into the Go port (reverted to PROSE 2026-07-12); this atom re-ports that scanner to Go. Steward verdict 2026-07-05 stands verbatim (from the original why): the framework is a guest in the host's home -- it lives strictly within the repository it was launched in; everything outside is sovereign and untouched absent an explicit user request. Trigger for the original rule: the operator once proposed setup_context_hook.py --patch-global --apply, which patched the global host configuration (a host-sovereignty violation that a host update overwrites anyway). EXACT MECHANICAL RULE (enforced by TestLaunchDirWriteScope_NoHomeWriteColocation in internal/selfcheck/launch_dir_scope_test.go): for every NON-TEST, NON-TESTDATA .go file under internal/ and cmd/hotam/, no function body may contain BOTH a home-directory reference (os.UserHomeDir(), os.Getenv("HOME"), os.Getenv("USERPROFILE"), or a string literal starting with "~/") AND a filesystem-write sink (os.WriteFile/Create/Mkdir/MkdirAll/Remove/RemoveAll/Rename/Symlink/Chmod); the conjunction within one *ast.FuncDecl body is the hit (same-function co-location, the scope within which a computed home path can flow into a write -- matches the spirit of the original Python scanner). os.OpenFile is deliberately excluded (read-or-write; the explicit write primitives cover the unambiguous case); os/user.Current().HomeDir is out of scope today (this repo imports neither, and would only matter under the same conjunction). The framework tree currently has ZERO home-directory references (verified 2026-07-13), so the scan is green today and fails RED the moment a committed function computes a home path next to a write sink. Refines R-work-within-launch-dir (the broad launch-dir principle, whose live-conduct residual carries the genuinely-unobservable half).
evidence: internal/selfcheck/launch_dir_scope_test.go::TestLaunchDirWriteScope_NoHomeWriteColocation (green on real repo: zero home-dir references in committed source)
internal/selfcheck/launch_dir_scope_test.go::TestLaunchDirWriteScope_DetectsColocation (non-vacuity control: synthetic co-location is flagged, lone home-ref and lone write-sink are not)

requirements.claim: `hotam apply-proposal` shall resolve a Conflict by its string ID when locating the Conflict node a proposal addresses, whether the ID is given directly or via a variable-style alias.
requirements.why: The C-8600b1b8 lesson: an earlier executable-graph design bound each Conflict's axis/context to local variables (c1_axis..c6_axis / c1_ctx..c6_ctx in domains/hotam-spec-self/graph.py), so literal-only matching in _find_conflict_call made every existing Conflict unaddressable and the standing P3 CONFLICT_STALLED action mechanically unlandable; the fix folded module/function-level `name = "literal"` assignments. This concern is structurally moot today: the graph is plain JSON data (graph.json), not executable source, so Conflict nodes carry plain string IDs with no variable indirection. findConflictIndex (internal/proposal/mutate.go) locates a conflict by exact ID match on the JSON graph -- no variable resolution is needed. This repairs the ACT half of the mediation loop for conflict transitions (R-active-loop-apply-tool). requirements.claim: Conflict.lifecycle shall admit a HELD(reason) state, entered only via a human-signed ConflictTransition, for tensions not resolvable by amending the member requirements.
requirements.why: Closes the design question in R-unresolvable-conflict-carries-variants (steward verdict, verbatim 2026-07-02): the axis 'turns into an entity if the contradiction cannot be resolved through changes in the conflicting parties'. HELD is that resting point, added to CONFLICT_LIFECYCLE as a QUIESCENT state (mirrors DECIDED/REVISIT_WHEN's shape exactly -- prefix-parsed, cyclic back to DETECTED on condition-fires) rather than a new node type -- the conflict itself does not change identity, only its lifecycle value. Entry requires the same signoff lock as DECIDED (check_held_has_decided_by, R-decided-needs-human-signoff applied to HELD) so an AI cannot silently classify a tension as unresolvable-by-members.

requirements.claim: A contradiction shall be modeled as a first-class Conflict NODE carrying axis + context + shared_assumption + steward, never as a `conflicts_with` edge between requirements.
requirements.why: The central ontological insight. An edge holds nothing; a node holds knowledge belonging to neither party (the axis, the context, the shared root) -- that is what makes contradictions first-class and clusterable. REPOINTED 2026-07-02 (Wave 7 move 4, P5 latent-connector cluster fix): assumptions moved from A-content-free-honest (an over-broad, unrelated content-freeness assumption shared by coincidence with two other unrelated requirements, producing a false 3-way latent-connector cluster) to A-conflict-is-a-node-not-an-edge, which names this requirement's actual premise.

requirements.claim: A Conflict shall be resolved only through in-graph changes -- amending its conflicting member objects and/or introducing an additional mediating entity that dissolves the tension -- never by a decision taken outside the graph.
requirements.why: Names the legitimate resolution surface (amend members, add a mediating entity, or both) so 'solved in the chat / in the code, conflict left dangling' is a visible violation of the connector-node discipline (R-conflict-is-connector-node). Steward verdict 2026-07-05: 'противоречия либо решают внутри конфликтующих объектов, либо дополнительным созданием сущности' -- the paths are not exclusive. Labeled INHERENTLY_PROSE 2026-07-10 (Этап T): the claim's essence -- that a resolution happened INSIDE the graph and not somewhere in the real world -- requires comparing the graph against the world, which no check_* can do; any 'enforcer' would only measure form (the R-boot-cite-measured form-metric theatre, REJECTED). The honestly-checkable remainder is already covered by check_decided_has_rationale_or_derived + the signoff checks (a DECIDED conflict must carry rationale-or-derived and a human signoff). This is permanent discipline, not closeable debt.

requirements.claim: At the CONFRONT step the operator shall check every change not only against SETTLED rules but also against the graph's goals, IMPLEMENTS aspirations and values, surfacing any contradiction before writing.
requirements.why: Steward verdict 2026-07-05, verbatim: 'кажется, не получится [таймер-будильник для IMPLEMENTS]. Это просто нужно модели помнить при внесении изменений - нет ли противоречий нашим целям, правилам, ценностям'. Context: the six-lens review flagged IMPLEMENTS aspirations as a silent corner (no aging, no fallout, no progress measure) and proposed a decay timer; the steward rejected the timer as unworkable -- aliveness of an aspiration is not measured by a calendar but verified at the moment of every change, by the operator's own conscience. So the duty lands on the CONFRONT step of the mediation loop: the confrontation scope explicitly includes goals, aspirations and values, not just SETTLED claims. INHERENTLY_PROSE: this is an operator-conscience discipline; the lexical confront tool cannot see semantic contradiction with values (its honesty boundary is already documented).

requirements.claim: The framework Go packages (internal/*, cmd/hotam/*) shall ship no business data (no example requirements, no example axes, no business stakeholders).
requirements.why: Atom of R-content-free-framework (no-business-data concern). D1 split decided by domain-user 2026-06-30. The framework packages (internal/*, cmd/hotam/*) ship no business data: all requirements, axes, and stakeholders live exclusively in per-domain graphs (domains/*/graph.json). Business data in the framework packages would couple them to a specific domain, violating content-free neutrality. requirements.claim: The methodology shall relieve an over-budget operator by splitting its domain into a bounded sub-domain owned by a spawned sub-operator (the horizontal lever).
requirements.why: SETTLED (P8): the P8 REFLECTION band fires 'over-budget' then crystallize first then if still over, delegate. The signal path is structural: check_operator_within_budget (internal/invariants/lifecycle_checks.go) detects the breach; ReflectOverBudgetOperators (internal/diagnose/finding.go) names the path in its Finding.imperative -- 'crystallize first (R-crystallize-before-split); if still over, delegate a sub-domain (R-context-bounded-delegation)'; `hotam what-now` composes these via the diagnose package. DomainScope narrowing (per-operator sub-graph) remains a later phase but the SIGNAL -- over-budget then delegate -- exists today. Makes the methodology scale-free; generalizes 'agent never lost' to 'agent never overloaded'.

requirements.claim: An operator's RESIDENT working set shall not exceed its context budget (measured by budget.measure — for CRYSTAL_CHARS the char-length of root CLAUDE.md vs the host cap), with any excess flagged as a structural OVERLOADED contradiction by the harness.
requirements.why: Built (P2): check_operator_within_budget fires when the operator's budget.measure exceeds limit. History: NODE_COUNT originally measured the crystallized SUBSTRATE (requirements+conflicts+assumptions), which R-working-vs-substrate-budget declares free — this falsely flagged operators as near-OVERLOADED for the very act of crystallizing or keeping REJECTED history. OP-director moved to CRYSTAL_CHARS (limit=150000, the observed host char cap) — the RESIDENT crystal (root CLAUDE.md) is now the thing actually metered, per R-working-vs-substrate-budget. DomainScope narrowing deferred to P5+. UPDATED 2026-07-13 (task #102): the CRYSTAL_CHARS value embedded in the LIVE-STATE "resident crystal N chars" line is now a fixpoint computed at generation time (generator.ComputeCrystalCharCountFixpoint: render → rune-count via utf8.RuneCountInString → re-render until the embedded number stops changing), not a read of a stale pre-existing CLAUDE.md from the previous run. Because the crystal embeds its own size, a non-fixpoint measurement never converged — two consecutive gen-spec passes produced different output — and the docs/gen/AGENT-CONTEXT.md LIVE-STATE was stuck at "0 chars" whenever gen-spec ran without --claude-md. The fixpoint is computed once per pass and feeds every LIVE-STATE carrier (root crystal + AGENT-CONTEXT.md + live-state.md) so they agree; the --claude-md flag now gates only whether the root crystal is written to disk. See TestComputeCrystalCharCountFixpoint_RealDomainGraph, TestGenSpec_CrystalFixpointConvergesAcrossRuns.

requirements.claim: The project-local hook (context_producer.py, wired via PostToolUse + Stop in .claude/settings.local.json) shall read a context-cache.json written by the global statusline script and write spec/.runtime/context.json.
requirements.why: REJECTED — REPLACES by R-work-within-launch-dir + the local-only context bridge. The claim required the project-local hook to READ a context-cache.json written by the global statusline script — i.e. it depended on patching/reading the sovereign host (~/.claude/cah-bin). Steward verdict 2026-07-05 (verbatim): 'cah вообще нельзя трогать - он ведь обновится всё сотрет. И он в праве быть собой, без наших вмешательств'. The --patch-global mechanism and the global-cache read were removed from setup_context_hook.py / context_producer.py; the honest local design is: context_producer reads ctx_pct ONLY from the local stdin payload, writing nothing when absent. This claim is now structurally impossible under R-work-within-launch-dir and is preserved for history. — (was: Design refined by the 2026-07-02 --patch-global investigation: the global cah-stamp/cah-status pipeline had NO existing on-disk cache for ctx_pct (rate-limits.json holds only 5h/weekly/effort, never context %) -- so this cannot honestly 'read the global cah-stamp cache' as originally worded. Instead spec/tools/setup_context_hook.py --patch-global (a separate, user-run, opt-in tool -- never applied automatically) surgically patches the global cah-status.js to ALSO write a sibling ~/.claude/cah-bin/cache/context-cache.json = {ctx_pct, model, stamp}; context_producer.py now reads that path (CAH_CONTEXT_CACHE override) as a fallback when its stdin payload lacks ctx_pct. Stays DRAFT: the patch exists and is tested hermetically, but no user has yet run --patch-global --apply against the real global script, so the end-to-end chain is not yet proven live. BUILD-TRIGGER unchanged: R-setup-claude-generates-settings has landed.)

requirements.claim: Every import in the core Go packages (internal/*, cmd/hotam/*) shall resolve to the Go standard library or the hotam module itself -- no third-party backend/runtime dependency.
requirements.why: Wave 1 mechanical-honesty pass (2026-07-02): R-backend-scope (PROSE, M37 steward verdict) makes the broader design-stance claim that the core stays backend-neutral 'by construction' and names no target backends -- a claim that is largely a prose non-decision (declining to build an OperatorBackend protocol), not itself machine-checkable end-to-end. But the CONSTRUCTION half of 'by construction' IS mechanically checkable: go.mod has zero third-party require directives (module github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpecGo, go 1.25.0, no dependencies), proving every internal/* and cmd/hotam/* import resolves to stdlib + the hotam module itself. Split out as its own atom so this narrow, mechanical guarantee can honestly reach ENFORCED while R-backend-scope itself remains PROSE. The automated AST import-scan enforcer is not yet implemented (historically test_backend_neutral_scope.py); the property is structurally verifiable via go.mod today. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestCoreImports_StdlibOrHotamSpecOnly in internal/selfcheck/imports_test.go mechanically verifies this claim by static source scan: every import path in every .go file under internal/ and cmd/hotam/ resolves to the Go standard library or the github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpec module (go.mod has zero require directives). settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Root CLAUDE.md shall contain a generated MEDIATION-LOOP sentinel block rendering the six-step input-processing loop -- ORIENT, LOCATE, CONFRONT, TRANSLATE, PRESENT, LAND -- each step naming its real tool command.
requirements.why: The loop is the operator's operating procedure for ANY input: it binds the role (guardian, hypothesis-checker) to tools that already exist -- `hotam what-now` (ORIENT), the Constitution index and generated docs (LOCATE), `hotam confront` + RECENTLY-REJECTED anti-relitigation scan (CONFRONT), Proposed* JSON in internal/proposal (TRANSLATE), steward decision (PRESENT, R-ai-presents-not-decides), `hotam apply-proposal` then `hotam gen-spec` then `go test` then closure via `hotam what-now` / `hotam all-violations` (LAND, R-verify-closure-per-action). Naming real commands keeps the loop executable rather than aspirational; a pass that writes nothing is a valid conclusion. The block is emitted by RenderMediationLoopBlock (internal/generator/claudemd.go) as static text baked in claudemd_static.go. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestRenderMediationLoopBlock_NamesSixStepsAndRealTools in internal/generator/claudemd_sentinel_test.go mechanically verifies this claim against the generated crystal on the fixture graph.

requirements.claim: The crystal generator shall render every object using a meaningful short form (an explicit summary, else its first whole sentence) instead of mechanically truncating text mid-word.
requirements.why: Steward verdict 2026-07-05: 'All that gets truncated must not be truncated but have a short version.' Mechanical truncation ([:96]+'...') creates mid-word stubs that look like knowledge but are illusions — the exact pattern this requirement prohibits. Implemented (task #103): the generator (internal/generator/claudemd.go) now renders short forms via shortForm(text, summary) — summary-priority (when a top signal's Target resolves to a Requirement carrying a non-empty Summary, that Summary is used), else firstWholeSentence(text), which splits on '.', '!', or '?' followed by whitespace or end-of-string and never cuts mid-word. The one violating call site — domainPulse's DOMAIN-MAP top-action pulse line, whose old runeTruncate form reduced '...See docs/gen/UNENFORCED.md.' to the mid-word stub 'See doc...' — is replaced; runeTruncate itself is deleted as now fully unused (its sole production caller was domainPulse; runeTruncateEllipsis is a separate, retained helper with a different single-'…' purpose). The Requirement.Summary field (internal/ontology/requirement.go) is consulted via summaryForTarget. Scope is deliberately tight: only the crystal-rendering site this requirement governs was converted; cmd/hotam/confront.go's runeTruncatePreview (a CLI diagnostic preview, not crystal rendering) is intentionally untouched. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: On overload, an operator shall crystallize first, re-measure, and delegate (split) only if still over budget.
requirements.why: SETTLED (P7): the order discipline is structurally bound. The apply-proposal mechanism (internal/proposal) crystallizes via Proposal types; the constitution (internal/generator/constitution.go, docs/gen/CONSTITUTION.md) names the ORDER explicitly. Splitting is for irreducible size, crystallizing is for un-offloaded knowledge; delegation is the lever of last resort. Splitting before crystallizing fragments knowledge that could have been freed in place. The over-budget REFLECTION finding (ReflectOverBudgetOperators, internal/diagnose/finding.go) renders the ordered imperative: 'crystallize first (R-crystallize-before-split); if still over, delegate a sub-domain (R-context-bounded-delegation).' The closure tool that would verify advancement before any split is considered is Planned (internal/methodology/tools_data.go). settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: An operator shall continuously crystallize working knowledge into requirement-code (the substrate) as the offload instrument, since crystallized knowledge does not count against context.
requirements.why: SETTLED (P4): the act of crystallization is now structurally supported. Every codified knowledge-piece flows through the proposal → apply → gen-spec → all-violations pipeline (internal/proposal Apply/ApplyBatch + cmd/hotam land.go). The discipline is made audit-able by that pipeline: each applied proposal is written atomically through applyToGraph (internal/proposal/apply.go), regenerated by gen-spec so docs cannot drift, and re-verified by all-violations before land reports success, so the WHAT is crystallized is not merely claimed but structurally verified at the feedback edge. The closure tool (per-action 'did the proposal remove its triggering diagnosis?') is Planned (internal/methodology/tools_data.go) — the land pipeline covers apply+regen+violation-check but does NOT yet run the per-action diagnosis-removal closure. STRUCTURAL (not ENFORCED) because WHAT to crystallize remains a steward call; the pipeline asserts HOW it is done. Implementation: internal/proposal + cmd/hotam/land.go. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Whether the decided_by steward signature shall be bound to a verifiable signal (git commit authorship, or a cryptographic signature) rather than remaining a free string trusted via review.
requirements.why: Steward verdict 2026-07-10 (Etap M): RESOLVED as decided_by stays a free string trusted via review -- the framework will NOT build a signature-verification mechanism of its own. Verifying that a signature is unforgeable (git commit authorship, a cryptographic identity) is the job of an EXTERNAL authorization/identity system; a content-free discipline framework only holds the SEAM for one, it does not become the verifier. That seam already exists and is named: ontology.Signoff (internal/ontology/signoff.go) carries an Instrument string field recording HOW a signoff was captured -- today 'personal' (SignoffInstrumentPersonal: a human approved it, trust the writer) or 'DEL-<n>' (SignoffInstrumentDEL: a filed delegation authorizes the decider), enforced against the SignoffInstruments allow-set. The reserved 'git'/'crypto' verifiable-signature values are NOT yet declared as constants in internal/ontology/signoff.go (the earlier docstring reservation has not been crystallized into the ontology), but the Instrument field itself is the landing seam: a future external system that supplies verifiable provenance changes only the VALUE of Instrument, never the SHAPE of the Signoff record. Prior OPEN context is preserved as one revisit trigger among others, not the sole reason: the value of a verifiable signature is not yet FORCED under a single-human-wears-all-hats regime (A-single-human-wears-all-hats) -- when a second live human steward appears, the question of whether one steward's free-string signature can be trusted sharpens again and this decision should be revisited then (ties to the role model, #44). The original OPEN was recorded 2026-07-05 after DEL-1 (the standing campaign delegation) closed, which restored the requirement of an explicit steward signature per conflict resolution and first raised the 'signature is a blank form' concern (six-lens review Enf#2 / Vision#1). settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: When an operator delegates a sub-domain, the sub-operator shall return only CONCLUSIONS with shared objects declared as an explicit border, never raw detail.
requirements.why: SETTLED (P8): the proposal protocol carries CONCLUSIONS (rationale + derived requirements) not raw context detail — the Proposed* types' narrow, typed API surface IS the contract (internal/proposal/types.go). The parent keeps the conclusion-as-proposal (internal/proposal Apply), not the file-dump of working context. Returning raw detail would re-import the sub-domain's whole context into the parent's budget, defeating the delegation. Implementation: internal/proposal (types.go ProposedRequirement/ProposedConflict/etc. + apply.go Apply/ApplyBatch).

requirements.claim: Every domain must contain a `director` agent at `domains/<name>/agents/director/` as the entry point operator.
requirements.why: The director is the domain's R-operator-acting-facet: it holds the crystal (CLAUDE.md), runs the boot sequence, and is the single entry point for any orchestrator. Without a director agent the domain has no defined operator and violates R-agent-never-lost. The name `director` is conventional, not arbitrary — it mirrors the OP-director role at the framework level.

requirements.claim: Every generated doc shall name its reader as an existing Stakeholder id in its header.
requirements.why: Stage E: a generated doc with no named reader is an important-yet-invisible artifact -- nobody is accountable for reading it, so drift between the doc and its audience's actual needs goes unnoticed (the generative law applied to doc plumbing, not just graph nodes). generator.DocReaderRoles (internal/generator/common.go) maps each doc kind to a portable ROLE hint (operator / domain-steward / framework-maintainer) and generator.ReaderHeaderLine resolves that hint against the DomainDocReaders binding (a code-level role->Stakeholder.id map: operator->ai-agent, domain-steward->domain-user, framework-maintainer->framework-author), stamping a `reader: <id>` line into every generated doc header (called from each Build* doc renderer: constitution.go, requirements.go, tensions.go, atoms.go, etc.). Resolution NEVER scans stakeholder ids for a role-shaped substring (R-doc-readers-declared-not-guessed): ResolveReader checks the explicit binding table, not a substring guess. The per-domain DOC_READERS manifest field is Not yet implemented: a single hardcoded DomainDocReaders map in common.go is used rather than a per-domain manifest field, so all domains share the same role->stakeholder binding today. check_doc_reader_resolves_to_stakeholder (internal/invariants/dangling.go) is an HONEST NO-OP: it would verify the binding resolves against the graph's Stakeholders, but that check needs the manifest binding (filesystem) which the graph-only invariant contract cannot access — the structural guarantee lives in the generator's ReaderHeaderLine instead. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Every domain's manifest shall declare a DIRECTOR agent that resolves to a real agent directory at `domains/<name>/agents/<DIRECTOR>/`.
requirements.why: A domain without a reachable director is an orphaned subtree: no operator can be loaded (R-agent-never-lost violated). The structural check prevents dangling DIRECTOR references the same way check_dangling_ids prevents dangling assumption ids. ENFORCED via check_domain_director_exists, which is an HONEST NO-OP: the invariant contract (Check func(*ontology.Graph) []Violation) operates on an already-loaded in-memory graph and has no access to the domain's directory path or manifest on disk, so it cannot verify that domains/<name>/agents/<DIRECTOR>/ exists (the historical Python invariant loaded manifest.py and checked scope.py on disk). The on-disk manifest.json (internal/loader) carries only self_hosting — the DIRECTOR field is not represented in the on-disk manifest; it lives in the graph's Operators/Goals and in the code-level knownDomainManifests map (internal/generator/claudemd.go). A future create_domain scaffolding layer (Planned, internal/methodology/tools_data.go) would carry filesystem-coherence checks of this kind. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: A new domain MUST be founded general-before-specific, not bottom-up from requirements, in 8 steps (the same authored-executable-specification canvas PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md Section 8 applies to a whole domain): (1) purpose, stakeholders, terminology MUST land first -- manifest purpose/goals plus Stakeholders plus Axes; (2) Process and its scenarios MUST land BEFORE any EntityType -- a Process node naming stages, roles, and drives_entities; (3) the object model, authored by hand in spec/model/ (all aggregates/value-objects and their fields, general before any one model's detail) MUST land BEFORE any operation; (4) operations, policies, and invariants, authored by hand in spec/application/ and spec/policy/, MUST land BEFORE any test; (5) executable tests, authored by hand, MUST land BEFORE the Requirements they prove; (6) Requirements, each linked to that authored code and those tests via implemented_by/verified_by, MUST land BEFORE Assumptions/Conflicts; (7) Assumptions and Conflicts MUST land BEFORE the final step; (8) generated documentation (REQUIREMENTS/PIPELINE/MODELS/TRACEABILITY/COVERAGE.md) and a coverage audit close the founding sequence. Each step MUST close with a regeneration (`hotam gen-spec`) and a check of the overview projection (PIPELINE.md, R-domain-overview-projection) before the next step opens. The diff against the domain's original source prose MUST run AFTER step 1 (purpose/stakeholders/terminology), NEVER only at the end of the founding sequence.
requirements.why: Steward finding this session (task #203, dogfooded on the `prat` pilot domain, PRAT-hotam/domains/prat): the methodology already defines WHAT a domain needs (manifest, Process, Axes, EntityTypes, Requirements, Assumptions/Conflicts -- each with its own SETTLED requirements and check_* invariants) and HOW to add each node kind (the six-step mediation loop, R-... per node kind), but nowhere states in WHAT ORDER a brand-new domain should be founded. The `prat` pilot paid for this gap concretely: its Requirements were landed before any Process node existed (the Process-node-for-stages pattern only arrived later, task #199/#200, see R-process-aspect-first's successors and R-domain-overview-projection/task #201), and its manifest stayed empty for an extended stretch (partially fixed by task #200; full fix tracked as tasks #210/#211, not yet done at authorship time). The compounding failure this produced: an agent asked 'what are the stages of the PRAT methodology' could NOT answer from the graph + generated docs alone and had to fall back to reading the original prose document directly (diagnosed and partially closed by task #205 / R-domain-overview-projection, commit 7520545, docs/GEN-CODE-CONTRACT.md, then docs/AUTHORED-SPEC-CONTRACT.md §9) -- a direct, observed consequence of building artifacts and rules before the skeleton (Process/manifest) existed to anchor them. This requirement originally named the wave order (skeleton -> artifacts -> rules -> tensions -> data, EntityInstances as wave 4). UPDATED task #234 (PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md, steward decision 2026-07-16, whole-file dated same session as the authored-spec discipline's own founding turnover): the framework itself pivoted from a code-generation discipline (an EntityType wave that produced generated Go structs/lifecycle methods, now retired -- see docs/AUTHORED-SPEC-CONTRACT.md §8 'History') to an authored-executable-specification discipline where an agent writes the domain's model/behavior/tests BY HAND (spec/model, spec/application, spec/policy, spec/tests) and Requirements carry implemented_by/verified_by links into that authored code (R-spec-link-embodied-vs-proven, task #223/#235). The old wave-4 EntityInstance step and the wave-1-as-generated-artifacts framing were defects of the code-generation era (I-2/I-3/I-4 from task #207's own retrospective) that dissolve naturally once founding order is re-expressed as the 8-step authored canvas PLAN §8 defines: object model (by hand) -> operations/policies/invariants (by hand) -> executable tests (by hand) -> Requirements linked to that code+tests -> Assumptions/Conflicts -> generated documentation. This UPDATE changes the claim's STRUCTURE (5 waves -> 8 steps, EntityType-generation language -> authored spec/ language) but preserves the original claim's SUBSTANCE unchanged: general-before-specific, skeleton (purpose/Process/Axes) before detail, regeneration+PIPELINE.md check at every step boundary, source-prose diff right after the skeleton step -- not a new requirement, a reworded restatement of the same discipline for the authored-spec era, refines-related to R-domain-overview-projection unchanged (see relations) and now sibling to R-authored-spec-layer-progression (task #235), which already explicitly refines this requirement's general-before-specific principle one layer down, into how authored spec/ CODE itself is built once a domain's Requirements already exist. Correctly landed as PROSE/feature-blocked (R-speculative-aspects-frozen's sibling pattern, the same honest-roadmap-debt shape this requirement always used): no domain-founding-wave-sequencer tool exists yet to mechanically enforce step ordering -- the claim is real and SETTLED today as operator discipline (the six-step mediation loop enforces WHAT and HOW per proposal; this requirement adds the missing WHEN/ORDER dimension a human or agent founding a domain must follow by hand until the sequencer exists), the enforcer is honestly not-yet-built roadmap debt, not a silent omission.
evidence: the `prat` pilot domain (PRAT-hotam/domains/prat) landed Requirements before any Process node existed for its own staged methodology -- the Process-node-for-stages pattern only arrived later via tasks #199/#200, the `prat` pilot's manifest stayed empty for an extended stretch, partially fixed by task #200, full fix tracked as not-yet-executed follow-on tasks #210/#211, an agent asked 'what are the stages of the PRAT methodology' could not answer from the graph + generated docs alone, diagnosed as R-domain-overview-projection (task #205, commit 7520545, docs/GEN-CODE-CONTRACT.md §7, now docs/AUTHORED-SPEC-CONTRACT.md §9)

requirements.claim: Every domains/<name>/ shall contain a docs/ directory which wraps the generated docs/gen/ plus any hand-written domain material.
requirements.why: The docs/ wrapper cleanly separates machine-generated output (docs/gen/) from hand-written domain material without requiring two separate top-level directories. Domain operators need a place to put domain-level notes, ADRs, and glossaries that are domain-specific and not governed by the framework generator. Keeping everything under docs/ mirrors the spec/docs/ structure established for the framework level. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestDomainHasDocsDir_RealDomains in internal/invariants/domain_has_docs_dir_test.go mechanically verifies this claim.

requirements.claim: Every `domains/<name>/` directory contains a `manifest.json` defining the domain's identity fields (id, description, goals, director), so the generator can discover a domain's metadata without loading its full graph.
requirements.why: The manifest is the machine-readable identity of the domain: it lets gen_spec and the root DOMAIN-MAP block discover a domain's metadata without loading its full graph. GOALS and DIRECTOR are required so any operator can reconstitute context from the manifest alone (R-agent-never-lost). ENFORCED via check_domain_manifest_valid (a thin delegator over five atomic sub-checks), all of which are HONEST NO-OPS: check_domain_manifest_exists_and_importable, check_domain_manifest_id_matches_dirname, check_domain_manifest_description_nonempty, check_domain_manifest_goals_nonempty, check_domain_manifest_director_nonempty (internal/invariants/domain_structure.go). They are no-ops because the invariant contract (Check func(*ontology.Graph) []Violation) operates on an already-loaded in-memory graph and cannot reach the domain's manifest on disk. Additionally, the on-disk manifest.json (internal/loader) carries ONLY self_hosting — the ID/DESCRIPTION/GOALS/DIRECTOR fields are NOT represented in the on-disk manifest; the DOMAIN-MAP block's presentation fields are sourced from a code-level knownDomainManifests map keyed by directory name (internal/generator/claudemd.go), with em-dash fallback for unknown domains. A future create_domain tool (Planned) would persist these fields and provide the filesystem-coherence enforcement layer. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: A business domain is represented as a directory at `domains/<name>/`.
requirements.why: Separates framework code (internal/, cmd/, the shared immutable framework body) from business content (domains/<name>/, per-business). Enables a future create_domain tool to scaffold new businesses without touching the framework body. Builds on the agent-as-directory pattern: just as agents are directories with their own CLAUDE.md+tools+agents (in the full layout), domains are directories with their own graph.json+manifest.json+docs/gen/. ENFORCED via check_domain_manifest_exists_and_importable, which is an HONEST NO-OP (it cannot reach the filesystem from the graph-only invariant contract); the structural guarantee that a domain is a discoverable directory is upheld by the loader (internal/loader/loader.go LoadGraph expects domains/<name>/graph.json) and the DOMAIN-MAP renderer (internal/generator/claudemd.go RenderDomainMapBlock walks domains/ on disk). settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: The root `CLAUDE.md` shall contain a DOMAIN-MAP block listing every `domains/<name>/` with id, description, goals, director, path, atoms-count.
requirements.why: The framework operator must be able to discover all domains without reading each domain's graph. The DOMAIN-MAP block is generated by RenderDomainMapBlock (internal/generator/claudemd.go) wrapped in the DOMAIN-MAP sentinel block by the root CLAUDE.md renderer, so it cannot drift from the actual directory layout (R-deterministic-generation): the renderer walks the domains/ directory on disk, loads each domain's graph.json for the SETTLED atom count, and looks up presentation fields (id, description, goals, director) from the code-level knownDomainManifests map. atoms-count gives the operator a quick load-budget estimate (R-context-budget-rule). The on-disk manifest.json carries only self_hosting, so the description/goals/director fields are sourced from knownDomainManifests (with em-dash fallback for unknown domains) rather than the manifest file; a future create_domain tool would persist them on disk. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestRenderDomainMapBlock_KnownDomainCarriesAllSixFields in internal/generator/claudemd_coverage_test.go mechanically verifies this claim against the generated crystal on the fixture graph. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: The root CLAUDE.md DOMAIN-MAP block shall carry, for every domain, an 'open actions' line stating that domain's open-action count and top action, and emit_cipher shall surface the aggregate open-action count of all non-pinned domains in the injected pulse.
requirements.why: Before this atom the root LIVE-STATE cipher diagnosed only the PINNED self-host domain, so a business domain's DETECTED conflict (e.g. hotam-dev C-ec1ec532 on axis speed-vs-verification) was INVISIBLE from the root crystal and the banner 'every contradiction is visible' was false at the altitude of the whole repo. gen_spec._domain_pulse runs what_now.diagnose per domain into the DOMAIN-MAP (the first eye); emit_cipher._other_domains_open sums the non-pinned domains' open actions into the pulse (the second eye). Both are generated, so they cannot drift from real graph state (R-deterministic-generation). Refines R-domain-map-generated. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestRenderDomainMapBlock_EntryCarriesOpenActionsPulse in internal/generator/claudemd_coverage_test.go mechanically verifies this claim against the generated crystal on the fixture graph. This enforcement covers only the DOMAIN-MAP open-actions pulse line; the claim's 'emit_cipher' wording is separately-tracked stale phrasing from the Python era (no Go emit_cipher mechanism exists) and is not asserted here.
