requirements.claim: Every domain MUST answer the overview question ("how is this domain structured as a whole") with ONE generated projection (OVERVIEW/PIPELINE.md), assembled from Process nodes plus EntityType plus the pipeline references between entities. This projection ALWAYS speaks in the language of the methodology/business-domain author: stage names are taken verbatim from Process.steps as authored, and any Go/code identifier appears ONLY as an adjacent annotation, never as a replacement for the author's own name. The projection's regenerability is the mechanical completeness test against the domain's original prose description: a stage or concept present in the source prose that does not surface anywhere in the projection is a discoverable coverage defect, not a silent gap.
requirements.why: Steward finding this session (dogfooded on hotam-spec-self itself): the current substrate (graph + gen-spec docs) is memory FOR CHANGE — confront answers 'what does this contradict,' trace/HISTORY.md answers 'why was this rejected before,' what-now/UNENFORCED.md answers 'what is in debt now.' It is NOT memory FOR UNDERSTANDING — an overview answering 'how is this whole methodology/domain structured, stage by stage.' Verified concretely: an agent asked 'what are the stages of the PRAT methodology' could not answer better from the graph + generated docs than from reading the original prose document directly — no generated projection anywhere assembles Process.steps + EntityType + pipeline edges into one whole-structure view. Diagnosis has two halves. (a) The ontology already carries a §Process type for exactly this (Lifecycle + Steps + roles_required + drives_entities, internal/ontology/process.go) and hotam-spec-self itself uses it (PR-closed-loop, the ORIENT-LOCATE-CONFRONT-TRANSLATE-PRESENT-LAND process instantiated as data) -- but the `prat` business domain (PRAT-hotam/domains/prat) has declared zero Process nodes, so its own staged methodology (e.g. the OTT/GPSM stage sequence) has no typed anchor at all, only prose. (b) `hotam gen-spec` only emits confrontation-shaped docs (REQUIREMENTS.md, HISTORY.md, UNENFORCED.md, TENSIONS.md, ...) -- there is no generated projection of the whole across Process + EntityType + pipeline links. This requirement records the target state; building the generator (OVERVIEW/PIPELINE.md, assembled from Process nodes + EntityType + pipeline references) is tracked as follow-on task #201 and is deliberately NOT implemented by this requirement -- correctly PROSE/feature-blocked (R-speculative-aspects-frozen's sibling pattern: the claim is real and SETTLED, the enforcer does not exist yet, so it is honest roadmap debt, not a silent omission). Regenerability-as-completeness-test mirrors the same principle already SETTLED for gen-code's requirement-atom coverage audit (GEN-CODE-CONTRACT.md §3.1, steward decision 2026-07-15): 'atom found' is not 'claim fully covered' there; here, 'a doc got generated' is not 'the whole structure is represented' -- both demand a mechanical diff against the original source (claim text there, prose document here), not a presence check. The language-fidelity clause (stage names verbatim from Process.steps, Go identifiers as annotation only) is the same discipline already SETTLED for R-crystal-carries-short-form and the zero-Cyrillic-in-code / audit-carries-original-text split in GEN-CODE-CONTRACT.md §1.1: the business author's own words are the primary artifact, code-facing names ride alongside, never replace them.
evidence: internal/ontology/process.go defines Process/Step/Lifecycle but the `prat` business domain (PRAT-hotam/domains/prat) declares zero Process nodes for its own staged methodology

hotam gen-spec output (domains/hotam-spec-self/docs/gen/*.md) contains no projection assembling Process.steps + EntityType + pipeline references into a single whole-structure overview — confirmed by directory listing (REQUIREMENTS.md, HISTORY.md, UNENFORCED.md, TENSIONS.md, CONSTITUTION.md, FRAMEWORK-INVARIANTS.md, GLOSSARY.md, REPO-MAP.md, live-state.md, AGENT-CONTEXT.md — all confrontation/status-shaped, none overview-shaped)

requirements.claim: Each `domains/<name>/docs/gen/` shall hold only the markdown generated from that domain's graph, with no cross-domain doc files.
requirements.why: Generated docs are the human-readable shadow of the graph (R-deterministic-generation). Keeping them inside the domain directory ensures each operator's boot sequence reads only its own REQUIREMENTS.md/TENSIONS.md/OPEN.md, not a mixed-domain dump. The anti-drift meta-test must be domain-scoped accordingly. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestDomainOwnsDocsGen_NoForeignOrOrphanFiles in internal/generator/docs_gen_ownership_test.go mechanically verifies this claim.

requirements.claim: Each `domains/<name>/` owns its own graph store (`graph.json`) defining its requirement/conflict/assumption graph, loaded and written via the shared loader.
requirements.why: Isolates each domain's requirement graph from all others. The single-file convention is `graph.json` loaded by loader.LoadGraph / written by loader.WriteGraph (internal/loader/loader.go), with each domain carrying its own domains/<name>/graph.json; the loader (marshalCanonical/atomicWriteFile) provides canonical serialization and atomic writes. The domain tooling reuses the same loader with a path argument (graphPathForDomain in cmd/hotam/common.go). Cross-domain references are explicitly forbidden: a requirement is local or it is a shared framework node. NOTE: the requirement ID retains its historical '-graph-py' suffix (the graph store was once spec/content/graph.py; renaming the ID is a separate operation) — the substance it names is domain ownership of its own graph store, today realized as graph.json + internal/loader. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Each `domains/<name>/` shall materialize a `tools/` directory (private tools) and an `agents/` directory (sub-operators) only when a real sub-tool or sub-agent is actually created — not eagerly scaffolded; a domain with no sub-tools/sub-agents yet correctly carries neither directory.
requirements.why: tools/ holds domain-specific scripts that must not pollute the shared cmd/hotam body. agents/ is the recursive sub-operator tree (R-agent-is-recursive-director). UPDATED 2026-07-13 (task #113, decision brief §3): reworded from 'shall contain both tools/ and agents/, even if empty' to lazy-materialization semantics — these directories shall materialize only when a real sub-tool/sub-agent is actually created, not eagerly scaffolded for every domain. A domain with no sub-tools/sub-agents yet correctly carries NEITHER directory; their absence is the correct state, not a violation. This extends the already-SETTLED lazy-materialization precedent R-claude-md-consolidates-when-single-agent ('Sub-agent crystals materialize only at real spawn time' — CLAUDE.md Recursion section) to the tools/ and agents/ subdirs. The eager always-true invariant is retired: the honest no-op checks (check_agent_has_agents_subdir / check_agent_has_docs_subdir / check_agent_has_tools_subdir in internal/invariants/domain_structure.go) operate on the in-memory graph and cannot reach the filesystem, and inventing the first filesystem-touching invariant check to enforce eager PRESENCE is a category change not worth the low-value structural nicety (decision brief §3 option (b) over (a)/(c)). Enforcement downgraded STRUCTURAL -> PROSE: the reworded claim describes a lazy/conditional state (absence is correct), so no meaningful check enforces eager presence; a future filesystem-aware check verifying 'dirs present iff a sub-agent/sub-tool exists' is theoretically buildable (hence enforceability stays ENFORCEABLE, not INHERENTLY_PROSE) but out of scope today.

requirements.claim: Framework-jurisdiction invariants (frameworkScopedInvariantNames) shall run only against a graph whose manifest declares self_hosting=true.
requirements.why: Wave 7 docrystallization of the gap named by the Wave 6/prior agent's landed bijection work: ontology.Graph.SelfHosting (internal/ontology/graph.go) is populated at load time by loader.resolveSelfHosting (internal/loader/loader.go), which reads the sibling manifest.json's self_hosting field (default false when absent or unreadable); invariants.AllViolations (internal/invariants/all_violations.go) gates the frameworkScopedInvariantNames set on this flag so those invariants fire ONLY when g.SelfHosting is true -- domains/hotam-spec-self/manifest.json sets {"self_hosting": true} (it IS the framework modeling itself); any other business domain (e.g. domains/hotam-dev/manifest.json, which does not set self_hosting) defaults to false and is correctly exempt from framework-internal plumbing checks that have no bearing on business content. The frameworkScopedInvariantNames set covers the domain/agent/tool-plumbing structural checks that only make sense against the framework's OWN self-model (check_domain_manifest_*, check_domain_director_exists, check_agent_has_*_subdir, check_bijection_r_to_enforcer, check_method_matches_docstring, check_rules_as_data_classification_coherent, check_constituting_not_in_unresolved_conflict). Without this gate, framework-jurisdiction checks would fire as phantom P1 violations against business domains that never claim to model the framework itself. Evidence: internal/loader/loader.go resolveSelfHosting; internal/ontology/graph.go Graph.SelfHosting field; internal/invariants/all_violations.go frameworkScopedInvariantNames set + the `if scoped && !g.SelfHosting { continue }` gate; domains/hotam-spec-self/manifest.json {"self_hosting": true}. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestFrameworkScopedInvariantsRunOnlyWhenSelfHosting in internal/invariants/self_hosting_gate_test.go mechanically verifies this claim. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: The generated docs/gen/*.md and graph.json shall equal the regeneration of the current graph, byte-for-byte.
requirements.why: Anti-drift meta-test — direct lift of dev-coin's pattern. The human layer (docs/gen) cannot be hand-edited without regeneration catching the drift. ENFORCED by TestGenerator_GraphJSONDoubleRegenerateIsIdentical (internal/generator/determinism_test.go), which rebuilds graph.json twice from the same in-memory graph and asserts byte-identity; its sibling TestGenerator_DoubleRegenerateIsIdentical covers every generated doc (Requirements, Tensions, Open, Unenforced, Glossary, History, Decisions, Constitution, Entities, RepoMap, FrameworkInvariants, LiveState, Atoms*). The generators are pure functions of (graph, today) — sorted iteration via internal/loader/sort.go, no hidden time/random sources — so regeneration over an unchanged graph AND an unchanged explicit today value always reproduces the committed bytes. UPDATED 2026-07-13 (task #96, external-review P0): BuildLiveState/BuildAgentContext/RenderClaudeMDFromTemplate and their transitive callees (diagnose.DiagnoseSignals, diagnose.FreshnessSignals) formerly called time.Now() internally to compute today's date embedded in freshness/status text, which made two regenerations on different calendar days non-identical even with zero graph changes — a hole in this requirement's byte-identity guarantee that let the committed root CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md drift (233/116/91 vs the graph's real 236/160/43) without CI's regen-idempotency check reliably catching it, since a same-day local regen looked clean while the next day's regen would show date-only noise. Fixed by threading today as an explicit parameter through every affected generator/diagnose function (`hotam gen-spec` gained a `--today YYYY-MM-DD` flag, defaulting to time.Now() only at the CLI edge when omitted, matching the existing hotam status/due/land/apply-proposal convention) — see TestBuildLiveState_TodayIsInjectable, TestBuildLiveState_SameTodayIsByteIdentical, TestBuildAgentContext_TodayIsInjectable, TestBuildAgentContext_SameTodayIsByteIdentical, TestRenderClaudeMDFromTemplate_SameTodayIsByteIdentical, TestDiagnoseSignals_TodayIsInjectable, TestDiagnoseSignals_SameTodayIsByteIdentical, and the CLI-level TestGenSpec_SameTodayIsByteIdenticalIncludingCrystal. The byte-identity claim now holds precisely as originally stated once today is held fixed — CI's regen-idempotency step regenerates the root crystals from the committed graph and diffs against the committed tree in one pipeline run (same real-world moment, hence same --today), so it is unaffected by this fix in the common case, but the injectability closes the previously-impossible 'pin a golden fixture and compare byte-for-byte on any day' path and eliminates the class of same-day-only false negatives. UPDATED 2026-07-13 (task #102): a SECOND byte-identity hole, independent of today-threading, lived in the SAME "resident crystal N chars" LIVE-STATE line: cmd/hotam/gen_spec.go computed the embedded count by reading the PRE-EXISTING CLAUDE.md on disk (written by the previous run) BEFORE regenerating it, so pass 2 embedded pass 1's file size into a differently-sized file — the line changed every gen-spec pass and never converged, which is exactly why CI's regen-idempotency check (regen twice, diff) was permanently red. The AGENT-CONTEXT.md code path never computed a real count at all (stuck at "0 chars" when --claude-md was omitted). Fixed by making the measurement a fixpoint (generator.ComputeCrystalCharCountFixpoint) computed unconditionally per pass and shared by every LIVE-STATE carrier — see TestComputeCrystalCharCountFixpoint_RealDomainGraph, TestGenSpec_CrystalFixpointConvergesAcrossRuns, TestGenSpec_CrystalCharCountIsRenderedFixpoint. The byte-identity claim now holds for two consecutive gen-spec passes over the same tree, which is precisely what CI checks.

requirements.claim: When the active domain has no content yet (empty graph), `hotam what-now` shall render a calm 'no content yet'-style signal, not an error.
requirements.why: Atom of R-empty-content-is-legitimate (calm-banner concern). D2 split decided by domain-user 2026-06-30. WHY: an error on missing content scares off new adopters who have not populated their domain yet. The calm behavior holds PARTIALLY: what-now over an EMPTY-but-present graph (0 nodes, graph.json loads successfully) returns 'none â€” graph clean' via formatSignals (cmd/hotam/what_now.go) â€” it does not error on an empty graph, which is the spirit of this requirement. However the 'missing graph.json' case (a freshly cloned framework with no graph.json at all) is NOT yet handled with a calm banner: loader.LoadGraph (internal/loader/loader.go) returns a read error and what-now propagates it rather than rendering a welcoming notice. The historical tools/what_now.py calm-banner path (test_main_empty_content_prints_calm_banner) is the reference design for implementing the missing-file calm path. The empty-graph case is covered structurally; the missing-file case remains an honest gap. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestEmptyContentCalmBanner in internal/diagnose/empty_content_test.go mechanically verifies this claim: DiagnoseSignals over an empty graph yields zero signals without panicking, and TopAction renders the calm 'none — graph clean' signal rather than an error. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: When the active domain has no content yet (missing graph), `hotam gen-spec` shall emit a 'no content yet' notice into docs/gen/*.md, not fail.
requirements.why: Atom of R-empty-content-is-legitimate (gen-notice concern). D2 split decided by domain-user 2026-06-30. WHY: failing on missing content would block the regen step of the closed loop for new adopters. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: genSpec (cmd/hotam/gen_spec.go) now loads the domain graph via loadGraphOrEmpty, which detects os.IsNotExist on the missing graph.json specifically and substitutes an empty graph (&ontology.Graph{DomainDir: domainDir}) instead of propagating the raw read error. An empty graph flows through every generator unchanged: the Build* helpers in internal/generator already detect g.IsEmpty() and render the calm EmptyNotice (internal/generator/common.go) into docs/gen/*.md, so the missing-file case produces output identical to the empty-but-present case (the two situations are indistinguishable to an adopter with nothing modeled yet). The discrimination is mechanical and the only one that should trigger the calm path: errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) — a genuine error (a malformed graph.json decode failure, or a permissions error) still surfaces as a real error, enforced by the TestGenSpec_MissingGraph_MalformedStillErrors non-vacuity control. The identical missing-file gap also exists in the what-now code path (R-empty-content-calm-banner's own why documents this); that is NOT this requirement's anchor and remains an honest open gap there — loadGraphOrEmpty is deliberately scoped to gen-spec, not shared via loadDomainGraph, to avoid expanding scope across every loader caller.
evidence: cmd/hotam/gen_spec_test.go::TestGenSpec_MissingGraphRendersCalmNotice — genSpec against a temp dir containing NO graph.json returns no error and renders the calm 'No domain content loaded' notice into docs/gen/REQUIREMENTS.md, mirroring the empty-but-present case. TestGenSpec_MissingGraph_MalformedStillErrors is the non-vacuity control: a malformed graph.json (decode error, not os.IsNotExist) still propagates as a real error, proving the calm path is specific to the missing-file case.

requirements.claim: A freshly-cloned framework with an empty graph shall pass all structural invariants â€” an empty graph is well-formed.
requirements.why: Atom of R-empty-content-is-legitimate (well-formedness concern). D2 split decided by domain-user 2026-06-30. WHY: if an empty graph is malformed, the framework punishes adopters for having nothing to model yet. This holds structurally: loader.validateGraph (internal/loader/loader.go) iterates over each slice and checks per-element fields â€” an empty graph (zero axes, stakeholders, assumptions, requirements, conflicts, operators, goals, entity types, entities) has nothing to violate, so it loads successfully; likewise invariants.AllViolations (internal/invariants/all_violations.go) over an empty graph returns zero violations because every check iterates an empty slice. An empty graph is well-formed by construction. There is no dedicated empty-graph-wellformed test, but the property is exercised by the loader/generator test fixtures that build minimal graphs and assert zero violations (the historical test_empty_graph_is_wellformed asserted the same property). ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestEmptyContentWellFormed in internal/invariants/empty_content_test.go mechanically verifies this claim: AllViolations over a completely empty ontology.Graph returns zero violations (an empty graph is well-formed by construction). settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: A requirement shall carry an enforceability kind from the set ENFORCEABLE or INHERENTLY_PROSE, distinguishing real closeable debt from permanent discipline.
requirements.why: The enforcement-gradient P0 REFLECTION conflated two categories: requirements that could have a check_* but don't yet (real debt) and requirements that are fundamentally judgment calls no check_* could ever verify (permanent discipline). Without this distinction the debt metric never converges -- inherent-prose rules were counted as debt forever. Splitting the two makes the P0 REFLECTION an honest, closeable signal.

requirements.claim: Every SETTLED/ENFORCED requirement's enforced_by entries shall resolve to a concrete enforcer — a registered check_* invariant or a real top-level Test* function under internal/**/*_test.go — via the shared enforcer-resolution logic.
requirements.why: Wave 1 (seed coherence audit): check_enforced_names_invariant (internal/invariants/enforcement.go) only checks that enforced_by is NON-EMPTY for an ENFORCED requirement -- a real typo in an entry (a renamed test, a stale check_* name, a leftover pytest-style node-id like test_x.py::test_y from an earlier implementation) passes that check silently and is only discovered when someone runs `hotam gate` by hand and it fails closed to the full suite. That is an honest but SILENT tax on the T1 tiered gate (R-verify-closure-per-action): the graph claims a targeted enforcer exists when it does not, and the steward never sees this as debt. check_enforced_by_resolvable (internal/invariants/enforcement.go) makes the debt visible: it resolves every check_* entry against the All registry (graph-local, no filesystem), and resolves every other entry against the set of real top-level Test* function names under internal/**/*_test.go via gate.TestFuncNames (internal/gate/gate.go walkTestFuncs) — the SAME scan internal/gate uses for targeted test selection — firing a Violation naming the exact unresolvable string. This is the regression guard for the wave-2 rebound that replaced 157 broken pytest-style enforced_by references with real Test*/check_* names. The companion check_enforced_by_test_has_teeth is an HONEST NO-OP: an AST body-scan that confirms a bare test_* is not an empty stub does not translate to a static graph-local check — a toothless test function either fails to compile or passes vacuously only until its invariant regresses (at which point `go test` catches it directly), so there is no need (nor graph-local way) to reproduce the historical AST scan. Resolvability (check_enforced_by_resolvable) and non-emptiness (check_enforced_names_invariant) remain load-bearing. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: A SETTLED requirement with enforcement ENFORCED shall be valid iff EITHER the engine mechanism (enforced_by non-empty) OR the authored mechanism (implemented_by AND verified_by both non-empty) names a real enforcer; neither present is a violation.
requirements.why: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §5 (steward decision 2026-07-16) and §12 make the ENFORCED gate DISJUNCTIVE: a requirement may be SETTLED without code as honest roadmap debt, but ENFORCED requires a real noun via one of two paths -- the pre-existing engine mechanism (enforced_by: a check_* registry name or a repo-wide Test* function, guarded by check_enforced_names_invariant's non-emptiness half) or the new authored-spec mechanism (implemented_by AND verified_by both non-empty, pointing into a domain's own spec/ tree). check_enforced_names_invariant alone only recognizes the enforced_by half and would wrongly reject a requirement legitimately ENFORCED purely via implemented_by+verified_by; check_enforced_requires_enforcer_or_authored_link (internal/invariants/authored_links.go) is the widened disjunctive gate that accepts either path, so a self-domain requirement enforced by the engine's own check_*/Test* machinery is not forced to fabricate a redundant authored spec/ reference, and a business domain's authored-spec requirement is not forced to invent a fake engine-side enforcer. Having implemented_by without verified_by (or vice versa) does NOT satisfy the authored path -- "embodied somewhere" without "proven somewhere" is not a real guarantee. Mutation-proof coverage in internal/invariants/authored_links_test.go proves both positive paths (engine-only, authored-only) pass, and a round-trip mutation (neither -> implemented_by-only, still violating -> both fields present, clears) proves the disjunction is exact, not a silent OR-by-accident.
evidence: cd D:/ai_dev/prat/HotamSpec && go test ./internal/invariants/... -run TestCheckEnforcedRequiresEnforcerOrAuthoredLink_MUTATION_NeitherMechanismFires -v : PASS (task #236 self-example W2, 2026-07-16); checkEnforcedRequiresEnforcerOrAuthoredLink (internal/invariants/authored_links.go) IS the disjunctive ENFORCED gate this requirement's claim states verbatim (engine mechanism OR authored mechanism, neither alone for implemented_by-only is enough); TestCheckEnforcedRequiresEnforcerOrAuthoredLink_MUTATION_NeitherMechanismFires is the mutation round-trip proving the exact disjunction: neither field set fires, implemented_by alone still fires, both implemented_by AND verified_by set clears -- proving the AND inside the OR is exact, not a silent looser check; enforced_by (engine mechanism, task #223) preserved unchanged -- this UPDATE adds the authored-path carrier alongside it, this requirement is now literally demonstrated by both paths it describes at once

requirements.claim: The enforcement level (PROSE / STRUCTURAL / ENFORCED) shall be a first-class Requirement field with enforced_by anchors naming the check_* invariant or Test* function that guarantees the claim.
requirements.why: M26. DECIDED 2026-06-30: Requirement.enforcement is a first-class field (not a derived report) since P6. The ENFORCEMENT_LEVELS constant set (EnforcementPROSE/STRUCTURAL/ENFORCED) is declared in internal/ontology/requirement.go (the EnforcementLevels map); check_enforced_names_invariant (internal/invariants/enforcement.go) validates that enforcement is a known level and that every ENFORCED requirement names a non-empty enforced_by tuple. A derived report would be inconsistent with check_bijection_r_to_enforcer (internal/invariants/self_reference.go) which requires the field to be authoritative (the bijection that every ENFORCED requirement's enforced_by names a real enforcer that names it back). Evidence: internal/ontology/requirement.go EnforcementPROSE/STRUCTURAL/ENFORCED constants + EnforcementLevels map + the Requirement.Enforcement/EnforcedBy/Enforceability fields; check_enforced_names_invariant + check_bijection_r_to_enforcer in internal/invariants; R-enforcement-levels-declared SETTLED.

requirements.claim: A host-hook guard shall deny direct Edit/Write to enforcement-perimeter baseline files (ratchet-test baselines, the active-domain pin), with sanctioned updates routed through a dedicated baseline-update tool.
requirements.why: Not yet implemented: the CLI (cmd/hotam) is a pure command-line tool with no host-hook / PreToolUse integration, so there is no guard mechanism that intercepts direct edits to baseline files. The reference design is a PreToolUse hook that denies Edit/Write to spec/tests/*_baseline.json and the active-domain pin, with sanctioned updates routed through a dedicated update-baseline tool (historically _graph_guard.py + tools/update_baseline.py). Without a guard on baselines, a ratchet test (e.g. atomicity) can be silently defeated by editing the baseline JSON to include the offending id -- the same self-judging-the-judge problem as unguarded graph.json edits (Enf#3, supervector A). PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: domains/<name>/docs/gen/ENTITIES.md shall be generated from the domain's graph by gen-spec, listing every EntityType with its lifecycle Mermaid diagram, fields, covering check_entity_* invariants, and instances.
requirements.why: Substrate-generates-operator extended to the entity layer. ENTITIES.md is the per-domain entity registry; the generator writes it deterministically (LF, utf-8) from the in-memory graph. Implementation: BuildEntities in internal/generator/entities.go renders the EntityType sections (Mermaid lifecycle diagram, field table, covering enforcers, instances) and is called by gen-spec (cmd/hotam/gen_spec.go) which writes the file to domains/<name>/docs/gen/ENTITIES.md. The invariant check_entities_md_lists_all_types is registered but is a NO-OP (it is a filesystem-coherence check that does not fit the pure-graph Check signature; the structural guarantee is carried by the byte-identity test layer instead, internal/generator/byteidentical_test.go). Anti-drift is enforced by byte-identical regeneration tests. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: The check_entity_* invariant family shall cover every declared EntityType by iterating g.EntityTypes, requiring no new check_* code per additional type.
requirements.why: Parametric iteration is what makes the entity aspect scale: one invariant covers all types today and tomorrow. Per-type invariants would grow the check surface linearly and force framework edits on every domain addition -- the inverse of the content-free design. checkEntityTypeLifecycleWellformed (internal/invariants/entity_checks.go) iterates g.EntityTypes directly, calling the shared lifecycleWellformedIssues function on each EntityType.Lifecycle; the same parametric-iteration pattern is used by every check_entity_* invariant (check_entity_instance_state_in_lifecycle, check_entity_instance_required_fields, etc.). Adding an EntityType to the graph requires zero new check_* code. Wave 1 mechanical-honesty pass (2026-07-02) promoted this from STRUCTURAL to ENFORCED once a demo fixture proved the iteration claim end-to-end; the iteration is directly visible in the check source and exercised by the entity_checks_test.go fixtures. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.why: Mirrors R-tool-is-its-own-requirement at the entity layer: an EntityType IS its own requirement; deleting it removes the R; changing its description changes the claim. Eliminates the prose gap between 'R about entity behavior' and 'EntityType implementing it'. renderEntityDerivedConstitutionSection (internal/generator/entities.go) projects each EntityType as a '- **R-entity-<slug>**' line into FRAMEWORK-INVARIANTS.md via BuildFrameworkInvariants (internal/generator/frameworkinvariants.go), listing the entityCoveringChecks family as enforced_by. The invariant check_entity_type_constitution_projection is registered but is a NO-OP (filesystem-coherence check that does not fit the pure-graph Check signature; the guarantee is carried by the byte-identity test layer instead). Dormant-but-real: 0 entity_types in the active domain today makes the projection section empty (rendered only when len(g.EntityTypes) > 0), not falsely enforced -- the guard is live and will project the moment a domain declares an EntityType. Wording precision (Wave 7 move 3a): entity-derived requirements are framework-plumbing, relocated out of CONSTITUTION.md into FRAMEWORK-INVARIANTS.md; the projection target is FRAMEWORK-INVARIANTS.md. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Each EntityType.Lifecycle shall be a Lifecycle value (the §Lifecycle keystone) with no parallel state machinery introduced.
requirements.why: The §Lifecycle keystone was introduced precisely so that every stateful aspect (Process, Entity, Goal, Operator) reuses one mechanism. Parallel state machinery would fracture the invariant surface and double the maintenance burden for each new aspect. The Lifecycle type is defined once in internal/ontology/lifecycle.go (State, Transition, Lifecycle structs); EntityType.Lifecycle embeds it directly (ontology.EntityType.Lifecycle is of type ontology.Lifecycle). The check check_entity_type_lifecycle_wellformed (internal/invariants/entity_checks.go) validates every EntityType.Lifecycle through the exact same lifecycleWellformedIssues BFS machinery that validates Process and Goal lifecycles -- no parallel state machinery. Wave 1 mechanical-honesty pass (2026-07-02) promoted this from STRUCTURAL to ENFORCED; the shared-keystone reuse is directly visible in the check source. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: The root `CLAUDE.md` shall contain only framework-level content, referencing domains exclusively through the DOMAIN-MAP block.
requirements.why: REJECTED — REPLACES R-claude-md-consolidates-when-single-agent. The claim 'root CLAUDE.md shall contain only framework-level content' contradicts the current single-file design: root CLAUDE.md now deliberately embeds the active domain's CONSTITUTION, EMBEDDED-THINKING, and EMBEDDED-TOOLS content directly (task #101), not merely a DOMAIN-MAP index. Domain-purity-of-root was the correct discipline under the old two-file (root + per-domain) model; under the consolidated single-file model it is superseded by the discipline that domain content lives in clearly sentinel-delimited generated blocks within the one file, per R-claude-md-consolidates-when-single-agent. — (was: Mixing domain atoms into the framework CLAUDE.md recreates the single-file coupling that domain isolation is designed to break. The DOMAIN-MAP block is a generated index (R-domain-map-generated), not inline content; every domain-specific atom stays inside domains/<name>/. ENFORCED in P17 by test_framework_claude_md_no_domain_atoms.)

requirements.claim: The framework body (`internal/` package tree) shall be owned by no single agent -- it is shared infrastructure any agent's code may import.
requirements.why: Second half of the split R-agent-imports-framework (R-requirement-claim-is-atomic). This is a non-code ownership/governance stance about the framework body -- distinct from the mechanically-checkable import-direction fact already split out as R-agent-code-imports-framework. No check_* can verify 'owned by no single agent' -- ownership is a governance convention (no per-agent CODEOWNERS-style claim exists anywhere in the repo, and none should), so this stays honestly STRUCTURAL/INHERENTLY_PROSE, carried by the fact that the framework package (internal/) has exactly one owner field (framework-author, the framework's own steward) and no agent directory declares or claims exclusive ownership of any internal/ module. The framework body is the `internal/` package tree (module github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpecGo; historically a Python hotam_spec.* package). Landed as the missing REPLACES target flagged in the Wave 2 review. requirements.claim: The framework shall generate thinking/*.md and tools/*.md deterministically from the methodology section registry and tool registry (the single source of truth).
requirements.why: Shared docs are the single authoritative reference for framework thinking and tool usage across all agents and domains. Generating them deterministically from the registries ensures they cannot drift from the code (R-drift-structurally-impossible). BuildThinkingDocs (internal/generator/thinkingdocs.go) renders one Markdown doc per methodology.Sections entry, and BuildToolDocs (internal/generator/tooldocs.go) renders one per methodology.Tools entry; both are called by gen-spec (cmd/hotam/gen_spec.go) which writes them to domains/<name>/docs/gen/thinking/<slug>.md and domains/<name>/docs/gen/tools/<command>.md. The registries (internal/methodology) are the single source of truth (historically the Python module docstrings and argparse --help output). requirements.claim: The framework test subset shall pass green under ANY active domain, or none, independent of which business domain is pinned.
requirements.why: Steward doctrine (verdict #8, verbatim): 'Бизнес всегда должен думать, что фреймворк работает. Быть рабочим — ответственность фреймворка.' The framework must PROVE its health without depending on a particular business domain's content. This is the DIRECTIONAL guarantee the tiering (R-framework-suite-tiered) exists to serve. Not yet implemented: the suite runs via `go test ./...` with no framework/domain tier partition -- there is no marker registry, no build-tag partition, and no mechanism to re-run the framework subset under a foreign domain pin. The proof-at-wave-boundary discipline is carried manually. (Historically the Python suite partitioned into 'framework' and 'domain' tiers via a DOMAIN_COUPLED registry in spec/tests/conftest.py, making the framework tier selectable via `pytest -m framework` and runnable under a foreign active-domain pin.) PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists; proving domain-independence requires a nested re-run under a foreign pin, not a single-run invariant. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Goal shall be its own first-class struct type (not a Requirement facet), with typed anchor 'GOAL-' and its own GoalLifecycle.

requirements.claim: A HELD Conflict shall carry at least two elaborated behavior Variants (id, behavior, implies, costs) as a payload field, not as new graph nodes.
requirements.why: Answers the attachment-shape half of R-unresolvable-conflict-carries-variants's open question ('payload field vs derived Proposed* pair'): Variant is a struct living in Conflict.Variants (payload), never a graph node -- anti-RDF, mirrors why axis/context/shared_assumption are Conflict fields and not their own node types. Variant is defined in internal/ontology/conflict.go (fields: ID, Behavior, Implies, Costs) and embedded as []Variant in the Conflict struct. check_held_has_min_two_variants (internal/invariants/conflict_decided_held.go) enforces len(distinct variant ids) >= 2 whenever lifecycle starts with HELD; check_typed_anchors_variant (internal/invariants/typed_anchors.go) enforces the V- prefix on every Variant id (R-anchor-everything extended to the new payload type) so a variant can be cited by reference (R-speak-by-reference) exactly like any other typed anchor. Additional HELD guards: check_held_has_nonempty_decided_by, check_held_by_is_known_stakeholder, check_held_by_not_member_owner. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: An agent shall receive its domain content from its initiator at boot and crystallize it into the domain code-spec (graph.json).
requirements.why: Resolves C-8600b1b8 (core-vs-aspect: R-content-free-framework vs R-agent-never-lost, shared assumption A-prose-suffices). Steward decision (domain-user, 2026-07-02), verbatim: «Агент должен получать от инициатора контент о своей области и должен его кристаллизовать в код-спеке». The framework itself stays content-free (R-content-free-framework unbroken -- it ships no business data) AND an agent dropped into a domain is never lost (R-agent-never-lost unbroken) because the INITIATOR (human steward or calling process) supplies domain content at boot time, and the agent's job is to crystallize that supplied content into the domain's code-spec (domains/<name>/graph.json) via the existing proposal pipeline (internal/proposal + cmd/hotam land), not to invent it nor to find it absent. This is narrower than R-crystallize-knowledge-to-code (which covers crystallizing an operator's own working knowledge in general, source-agnostic); this requirement pins down the SOURCE of domain content specifically as the initiator-at-boot, closing the core-vs-aspect tension. REQUALIFIED 2026-07-02 (Wave 2 honesty pass): previously carried default enforceability=ENFORCEABLE, which listed it as closeable debt in UNENFORCED.md -- but no check_* can ever verify 'the initiator supplied content at boot' as a runtime event (it is a fact about a conversation/process outside the graph's own reach, not a property of the committed graph state). This is the same honesty class as R-ai-presents-not-decides and R-two-altitude-ontology: a real, permanent discipline that is INHERENTLY_PROSE, not ENFORCEABLE-but-unbuilt.

requirements.claim: hotam gate (internal/gate.SelectTier1), when invoked for a target anchor, shall resolve the T1 targeted-enforcer subset from the target's enforced_by tuple (union of resolved check_*/Test* names) instead of requiring the full test suite for that lookup.
requirements.why: Reworded 2026-07-12 to describe what is actually implemented and tested: internal/gate.SelectTier1 (internal/gate/gate.go) correctly resolves a target's enforced_by into the union of concrete Go check_*/Test* enforcers -- TestSelectTier1_CheckNameResolves, TestSelectTier1_TestFuncNameResolves, TestSelectTier1_MultipleChecksUnion. The prior claim asserted this selection ran 'by default' inside the LAND pipeline (historically tools/apply_proposal.py consulting tools/gate.py) -- that automatic wiring was never carried through: hotam land (cmd/hotam/land.go) runs apply -> gen-spec -> all-violations and does not call gate.SelectTier1 at all; the selector is reachable only via the standalone `hotam gate <anchor>` command (cmd/hotam/gate_cmd.go). This is the honest, narrower claim the code actually guarantees. The lost intent -- wiring T1 selection as LAND's default verify tier, to avoid the full-suite time tax on every proposal -- is preserved as its own open item, not silently dropped: see R-t1-selector-as-land-default (DRAFT). Decision reached via agent consultation (fm), per R-ai-presents-not-decides / R-decided-needs-human-signoff, after an earlier content-refresh batch flagged the mismatch as a found-issue.

requirements.claim: internal/gate.SelectTier1 shall fall back to Confident=false (fail-closed, signaling the caller to run the full suite) on any selection uncertainty: an unknown/unresolvable target anchor, an empty enforced_by tuple, a Conflict target (no per-instance enforced_by), or any enforced_by entry that cannot be resolved to a concrete Go check_*/Test* function -- never returning a partial or best-effort subset in an uncertain case.
requirements.why: Reworded 2026-07-12 to describe the real, tested contract of internal/gate.SelectTier1 (internal/gate/gate.go), scoped to what the selector itself decides: TestSelectTier1_FailClosed_TargetNotFound, TestSelectTier1_FailClosed_EmptyEnforcedBy, TestSelectTier1_FailClosed_ConflictTarget, TestSelectTier1_FailClosed_PythonTestPathUnresolved, TestSelectTier1_FailClosed_BareTestFuncUnresolved, TestSelectTier1_FailClosed_PartiallyUnresolved, TestSelectTier1_FailClosed_CheckWithNoTestCoverage. The prior claim additionally asserted fail-closed behavior for apply()-level cases -- a ProposedRejection target, or the creation of a brand-new Requirement/Conflict node -- that were decisions historically made by tools/apply_proposal.py::apply before consulting the selector. Those apply-level cases are not implemented at all today (hotam land does not call the selector, so there is no apply()-level fail-closed decision to make); a ProposedRejection target that already exists in the graph would in fact resolve confidently via its own enforced_by, which is a real behavioral gap, not just a wording gap. That lost intent -- fail-closed treatment for rejection/creation cases once gate is wired into land -- is preserved, not silently dropped: see R-t1-selector-as-land-default (DRAFT). Decision reached via agent consultation (fm), per R-ai-presents-not-decides / R-decided-needs-human-signoff.

requirements.claim: hotam land shall leave a domain's graph.json and generated docs mutually consistent even when a later stage (doc regeneration or the post-regen violations check) fails after the proposal apply stage already wrote a new graph.json — a failure rolls back to the pre-land state rather than leaving the graph and docs divergent.
requirements.why: TaskList P1-77 / external review flagged a concrete bug in the pre-fix cmd/hotam/land.go:86: step (a) apply writes a new graph.json to disk, but if step (b) gen-spec or step (c) all-violations then fails, the command returned early and left that NEW graph.json sitting next to STALE (pre-land) docs/gen/*.md — the exact drift `hotam land` exists to prevent, now created BY a failed land instead of prevented by a successful one. The fix is a compensating-rollback design, not two-phase commit on the filesystem: cmdLand snapshots graph.json + graph.lock (snapshotGraphFiles) before step (a) ever writes; if step (b) or (c) fails, rollbackLand restores those exact pre-land bytes and then re-runs genSpec. This is sufficient (not just a partial fix) specifically because genSpec is a PURE function of the on-disk graph.json — it calls loadDomainGraph fresh every invocation and deterministically re-renders every doc from it, so restoring the graph and re-rendering restores the docs to the identical pre-land content too, without needing to snapshot docs/gen/*.md separately. A first-ever land into a brand-new domain (graph.json/graph.lock absent pre-land) rolls back to absence via restoreGraphFile's remove-if-absent path, keeping rollback idempotent and correct even at that boundary. Proven 2026-07-13 by three tests in cmd/hotam/land_test.go: TestCmdLand_GenSpecFailure_RollsBackGraphJSON forces a genSpec failure after a successful apply via a --claude-md path pointed at a directory, and proves graph.json is byte-identical to the pre-land baseline and graph.lock is removed again (pre-land state was absent); TestRollbackLand_RestoresFilesAndRegeneratesDocs unit-tests the rollbackLand helper directly, proving the docs are re-rendered to baseline content (not just the graph file restored) after a simulated post-apply, post-gen-spec failure; TestCmdLand_SuccessPathDoesNotRollBack is the regression guard proving the happy path still lands normally and does NOT spuriously roll back (graph.lock remains, new node present in both graph.json and docs) — together these prove the rollback fires exactly when needed and never when not. This is the SAME situation as the sibling R-land-tier-trace/-best-effort/-skips-dry-run requirements (also `hotam land` CLI-orchestration behavior), except those three remain genuinely unimplemented (no runtime land-log writer exists yet in cmd/hotam), so they stay PROSE correctly — this requirement is the one case among the wave-6 closeable-debt list where the enforcer already existed but the resolver could not see it. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestCmdLand_GenSpecFailure_RollsBackGraphJSON, TestRollbackLand_RestoresFilesAndRegeneratesDocs, TestCmdLand_SuccessPathDoesNotRollBack in cmd/hotam/land_test.go, resolver widened to cover cmd/ this session (task #97) — internal/gate.TestFuncNames (mechanism #1, the Test*-name half of enforced_by resolution) now walks both internal/**/*_test.go and cmd/**/*_test.go, so a real Go test enforcer living in cmd/hotam is no longer structurally invisible to check_enforced_by_resolvable / hotam gate just because of which directory it happens to live in. Mechanism #2 (the check_* literal -> tests map) stays scoped to internal/invariants only, per its own unchanged reasoning.
evidence: Re-ran `go test -run TestCmdLand ./cmd/hotam/...` on 2026-07-13 (the day after this requirement was created and ENFORCED in this same session, task #97): all 9 TestCmdLand* cases pass, including the three cited enforcers TestCmdLand_GenSpecFailure_RollsBackGraphJSON, TestRollbackLand_RestoresFilesAndRegeneratesDocs, and TestCmdLand_SuccessPathDoesNotRollBack in cmd/hotam/land_test.go — `ok github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpec/cmd/hotam 3.044s`.

TestCmdLand_GenSpecFailure_RollsBackGraphJSON forces a genSpec failure after a successful apply (via --claude-md pointed at a directory) and asserts graph.json is byte-identical to the pre-land baseline and graph.lock is removed again — this is the mechanical proof of the rollback half of the claim; independently reproducible by any future reader via the same `go test -run` invocation.

TestCmdLand_SuccessPathDoesNotRollBack is the paired regression guard proving the happy path does NOT spuriously roll back (graph.lock remains, new node present in both graph.json and docs) — together with the rollback test this closes the loop: rollback fires exactly when needed, never otherwise.

The enforced_by resolver fix landed this same session (task #97, internal/gate.TestFuncNames now walks cmd/**/*_test.go as well as internal/**/*_test.go) is what makes these cmd/hotam-resident tests visible to `hotam gate` / check_enforced_by_resolvable at all — confirmed by `go run ./cmd/hotam req show R-land-is-transactional --domain domains/hotam-spec-self --json` showing enforcement: ENFORCED with all three test names populated in enforced_by, not PROSE.

requirements.claim: The what-now harness shall render latent-connector suspects as one P5 action per shared-assumption cluster rather than one action per requirement pair.
requirements.why: Verified noise shape: 22 P5 lines were 21 pairs sharing one assumption plus 1 genuinely distinct candidate drowned by them. Clustering by the pairs' specific-shared-assumption signature renders the review surface at the size of the decision space while keeping every pair visible. ontology.LatentConnectorClusters (internal/ontology/graph_traversal.go) groups latent-connector pairs by their shared-assumption signature key, producing LatentCluster records (Assumptions, Requirements, Pairs). DiagnoseSignals (internal/diagnose/signal.go) renders each cluster as ONE PLatentConnector (priority 5) signal via the what-now harness, with the full pair list visible in the message. The same detector is reused by hotam inspect (InspectSharedAssumptionClusters in internal/diagnose/inspect.go). A threshold shift was rejected: it only moves the noise cliff. ENFORCED via TestLatentConnectorSuspectsAndClusters (internal/ontology/graph_smoke_test.go). settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Every non-empty Assumption.machine_check shall be a well-formed Python expression (compilable in eval mode), never free prose.
requirements.why: The machine_check field was carried by 2 of 12 assumptions ('python.version >= (3, 12)', 'len(graph.requirements) + len(graph.conflicts) < 10_000') and had NEVER been checked in any way — pure invisible substrate. check_assumption_machine_checks_syntactic compiles each non-empty machine_check in eval mode; a SyntaxError is a Violation. The honesty boundary is deliberate and documented (R-uncrystallizable-automated): it does NOT execute or assert-true the formula, because the two real formulas evaluate against DIFFERENT, not-yet-materialized namespaces ('python' is not a defined object as written; 'graph' expects a binding), and §Assumption states machine_check is 'carried but not run' (spec-stack layers 4/5 deferred). What is guaranteed structurally without inventing semantics is that the recorded formula is a compilable expression — the seam the deferred Z3/Hypothesis layers attach to — rather than prose masquerading as machine_check. Promoting to real execution is a separate later atom once a domain supplies the evaluation namespace.

requirements.why: REJECTED — premise requires installing/wiring a hook in the user's global ~/.claude configuration to emit context %, which R-work-within-launch-dir (SETTLED/ENFORCED) explicitly forbids ("never modifying anything outside it — including ... the global ~/.claude configuration"). This is the same steward-cited reason (2026-07-05: "cah вообще нельзя трогать — он ведь обновится всё сотрет") that already REJECTED the sibling R-context-hook-piggybacks-cah-stamp. An honest successor already exists and is SETTLED/ENFORCED: R-unmeasured-cipher-names-host-boundary, which measures context only when the local stdin payload honestly carries ctx_pct and otherwise reports UNMEASURED — exactly the scope this DRAFT wanted, without crossing the host boundary. Steward decision 2026-07-12, independently confirmed by two agents (crush and fm) reading the same graph evidence. — (was: tools/context.py reads spec/.runtime/context.json; the producing hook lives in the user's global ~/.claude settings (cah-stamp emits context %) — installing it is a STEWARD decision outside the framework body, so this stays DRAFT until the hook is approved and wired.)

requirements.claim: Each check_* function registered in the invariant registry shall carry a Claim/Rule/Why text that shares non-trivial lexical overlap with its Violation messages.
requirements.why: The audit principle 'description is the claim, body is the verification' applies here. The Invariant struct (internal/invariants/invariant.go) carries Claim, Rule, Why, and Check as FIELDS OF ONE STRUCTURE registered together via MustRegister -- the description and the logic are colocated in the same var block, authored and reviewed in the same diff, so the docstring-vs-body drift mode cannot arise. (Historically, in the Python implementation, each check_* function had a separately AST-parsed docstring whose RULE line was compared against Violation message string literals via Jaccard token overlap; the struct-colocation makes that drift mode architecturally inapplicable.) The registered check_method_matches_docstring (internal/invariants/self_reference.go) is therefore an HONEST NO-OP (returns nil): the drift mode it guarded against is inapplicable when description and logic are structurally bound in one struct literal. Coherence is instead enforced at code-review time and structurally by registry_complete_test (asserts no Claim/Rule/Why is empty). FOUND ISSUE (not fixed): enforcement is ENFORCED with a no-op enforcer -- the named check_method_matches_docstring cannot verify lexical overlap because it returns nil unconditionally. The enforcement/enforced_by are out of scope for this content batch. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Changes to domains/*/graph.json shall be made only through `hotam apply-proposal` / `hotam land`, with direct hand-edits prohibited outside of bootstrap events.
requirements.why: The closed loop's ACT half goes through the proposal writer (R-active-loop-apply-tool). The sanctioned write path is `hotam apply-proposal <file.json>` or `hotam land` (cmd/hotam/apply_proposal.go, cmd/hotam/land.go), which load a Proposed* JSON, validate it, mutate the in-memory graph, check for new invariant violations, and only then write graph.json + graph.lock (internal/proposal/apply.go). Not yet implemented as a deterministic host-hook guard: the CLI is a pure command-line tool with no PreToolUse / host-hook integration, so there is no mechanism that mechanically blocks direct Edit/Write on graph.json. (Historically the Python _graph_guard.py PreToolUse hook provided that mechanical block.) Without a guard on graph.json, a hand-edit can bypass the proposal protocol's validation+invariant-check gate -- the same self-judging-the-judge problem (Enf#3, supervector A). PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestNoHandEditGraph_RealDomainLocksPinCurrentGraph in internal/loader/lock_real_domains_test.go mechanically verifies this claim. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Operator epistemics (observations, beliefs, reasoning) shall live in the working dialogue, crystallized into the substrate only on request.
requirements.why: M21. Steward verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «нужно думать в чате. И по просьбе в чате записывать свои мысли» (English: 'need to think in chat. And on request in chat, write down one's thoughts'). This settles the M21 question against modeling belief-vs-reality drift as new first-class types: an operator's live reasoning stays in the session dialogue (the mediation loop's CONFRONT/TRANSLATE steps), and is crystallized into a typed node ONLY when explicitly asked to (mirrors R-crystallize-knowledge-to-code's on-demand discipline). REQUALIFIED 2026-07-02 (Wave 7 move 2 honesty pass): previously carried default enforceability=ENFORCEABLE, which listed it as closeable debt in UNENFORCED.md -- but no check_* can ever verify 'an operator kept its reasoning in the dialogue and crystallized only on request' as a property of the committed graph (it is a fact about a conversation, not the graph state). This is the same honesty class as R-initiator-supplies-domain-content: a real, permanent dialogue discipline that is INHERENTLY_PROSE, not ENFORCEABLE-but-unbuilt. RE-ATOMIZED Wave 8 move 2 (2026-07-03): the compound-claim audit (historically performed by the Python audit_atomicity tool, not yet implemented as a Go command) flagged the prior claim as COMPOUND -- it also asserted 'Assumption remains the only belief-carrying node type -- no separate Observation/Evidence types', which duplicates R-no-observation-type (SETTLED, STRUCTURAL); that mechanically-checkable half already lives there. Trimmed this claim to the dialogue-discipline half only (the genuinely INHERENTLY_PROSE half with no duplicate owner) -- R-no-observation-type remains the sole, undiluted owner of the negative-existence half. No semantic narrowing: both halves are still live exactly as before, now under two non-overlapping atoms. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: An Operator shall be a Stakeholder's ACTING facet: it owns a bounded DomainScope, carries a ContextBudget and capabilities, and may have a parent Operator.
requirements.why: REJECTED — REPLACES by R-operator-is-frozen-dataclass + R-operator-references-stakeholder + R-operator-has-context-budget + R-operator-may-have-parent per atomicity discipline (R-requirement-claim-is-atomic). The original claim mixed four concerns: type identity, stakeholder reference, budget, hierarchy.

requirements.claim: The framework's tools shall talk to the acting agent through a single OperatorBackend protocol (get_context_state / request_steward_approval / delegate), so the methodology does not hard-depend on which coding-agent or model drives it.
requirements.why: REJECTED -- REPLACES by R-backend-scope (SETTLED). M37 steward verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «не важно -- каждый умный агент под себя допишет» (English: 'it doesn't matter -- every smart agent will adapt it for itself'). The steward explicitly declined to build a designed OperatorBackend protocol (get_context_state / request_steward_approval / delegate): the core surface (spec/tools/*.py CLIs, JSON Proposed* shapes, pytest verification) already has no dependency on any one agent's runtime and stays backend-neutral BY CONSTRUCTION, not by an abstraction layer built ahead of a second real backend. Building the protocol now would be exactly the speculative-engineering-against-hypothetical-backends R-speculative-aspects-frozen already warns against. R-backend-scope (SETTLED, PROSE) already carries this verdict verbatim in its why and is the surviving atom; R-operator-backend-protocol is rejected rather than left DRAFT so the graph does not carry a dead aspiration as open debt. — (was: Today tools/ implicitly assume Claude Code (Agent tool, Bash, chat-steward). BUILD-TRIGGER: a SECOND concrete backend becomes real (CI runner, a different coding agent, or a programmatic steward). Until then, abstracting for hypothetical backends is the big-bang-up-front antipattern (weight ∝ cost of an unnoticed conflict). See OPEN R-backend-scope (which backends are real?).)

requirements.claim: The operator's CLAUDE.md shall embed the full content of its scope-relevant thinking documentation inline, not as markdown links, so the operator holds the methodology itself rather than a table of contents.
requirements.why: REJECTED — REPLACES by R-operator-crystal-embeds-thinking-distilled: full-text embedding contradicted R-crystal-reload-by-reference and breached the 150k host limit (CLAUDE.md reached ~200k chars); the crystal now carries a RULE+WHY distillate + Tier-3 pointer instead. — (was: A link the operator must separately fetch is a re-derivation tax on every turn; inlining the methodology content means it is present in the loaded substrate from the first token (R-operator-prompt-from-substrate). Task #98 (A1) built the EMBEDDED-THINKING block; ENFORCED by test_embedded_thinking_sentinels_present (sentinels exist) and test_embedded_thinking_contains_full_topic_content (content is the full topic text, not a link).)

requirements.claim: The operator's CLAUDE.md shall embed one RULE sentence per thinking topic (sourced from Section.Canon, guarded via shortForm) plus a path link to domains/<domain>/docs/gen/thinking/<slug>.md, not a multi-line RULE+WHY distillate.
requirements.why: REPLACES the Tier-1 multi-paragraph RULE+WHY distillation approach: even compressed RULE+WHY pairs consumed ~19k chars across 22 topics. The implemented design embeds one real RULE sentence per §-section, sourced from Section.Canon (internal/methodology/sections_data.go) -- the same registry BuildThinkingDocs (internal/generator/thinkingdocs.go) reads from, so Canon is the single source of truth for both the inlined RULE and the full thinking-doc on disk (domains/<domain>/docs/gen/thinking/<slug>.md). The thinking/*.md docs are themselves generated FROM this registry's Canon/Narrative/Why, so sourcing the inlined rule from Canon adds no new source of truth. Each Canon is guarded through shortForm(canon, "") (firstWholeSentence) as future-proofing against multi-sentence Canon drift (R-crystal-carries-short-form); today's 29 Canon values are single standalone sentences (2694 chars total, min 44 / max 178 / avg 93), so no truncation occurs and the short-form guard is a pure regression hedge. The rendered '- **§X** — <Canon>' block costs ~3.3k chars; the resident crystal moved 16458 -> 19335 chars (+2877), far below the 150000-char host cap (R-context-budget-rule, ENFORCED via ComputeCrystalCharCountFixpoint) and nowhere near the ~200k the OLD full-text embedding reached. Claim path/name fixes landed in this same proposal: corrected the Python-era 'short_form' name to the Go identifier 'shortForm', and the stale 'spec/docs/thinking/<slug>.md' path to 'domains/<domain>/docs/gen/thinking/<slug>.md' (matching the generator's path convention). ENFORCED 2026-07-13 via three tests in internal/generator/claudemd_coverage_test.go: TestRenderEmbeddedThinkingBlock_DistillsCanonPerSection (every registered section's short-formed Canon appears in the rendered block, bullet shape verified), TestRenderEmbeddedThinkingBlock_NoMidWordTruncation (no mid-word '...' stub for any current Canon value -- regression guard mirroring TestShortForm_NoMidWordEllipsis), TestRenderEmbeddedThinkingBlock_CrystalWithinBudget (crystal char-count fixpoint converges and stays under the 130000 warn threshold).
evidence: internal/generator/claudemd_coverage_test.go::TestRenderEmbeddedThinkingBlock_DistillsCanonPerSection — every registered methodology.Sections entry's short-formed Canon appears as a '- **§X** — <rule>' line in RenderEmbeddedThinkingBlock output, and the thinking-doc path pointer survives.

internal/generator/claudemd_coverage_test.go::TestRenderEmbeddedThinkingBlock_NoMidWordTruncation — the rendered block contains no '...' stub for any current Canon value (firstWholeSentence returns each single-sentence Canon whole).

internal/generator/claudemd_coverage_test.go::TestRenderEmbeddedThinkingBlock_CrystalWithinBudget — ComputeCrystalCharCountFixpoint converges against the fixture graph and the resulting crystal char count is under the 130000 warn threshold, a sanity check against the documented historical ~200k budget breach.

The discipline is structural via: the Director's Map is the crystal (CLAUDE.md); CONSTITUTION.md is generated from the SETTLED laws; the boot-sequence in §6 names the exact steps to reconstitute. Per the anchoring super-rule it cites code handles (R-/C-/§/file) so understanding is regained fast; the delegation hierarchy is therefore a TREE of CLAUDE.md crystals (exactly how Claude Code nests CLAUDE.md per directory), one per operator, each bounded by its context budget. requirements.claim: An Operator shall be a dedicated struct type in internal/ontology/operator.go carrying the typed anchor 'OP-', with field mutations performed only through the proposal system.
requirements.why: Atom of R-operator-acting-facet (type identity concern). Operator is a plain struct (internal/ontology/operator.go: fields ID, Stakeholder, Lifecycle, ContextBudget, Parent *string, Scope, Why, DeclOrder). Go has no frozen-dataclass language primitive (historically Python's @dataclass(frozen=True) provided one), so immutability is a DISCIPLINE, not a structurally-enforced guarantee: there are no setter methods, and field-level mutations are performed only through the proposal system (ProposedOperatorBudget.mutate in internal/proposal/mutate.go re-writes g.Operators[idx].ContextBudget). The typed-anchor discipline ('OP-' prefix) is enforced by check_typed_anchors_operator (internal/invariants/typed_anchors.go). OP-director is the first instance. requirements.claim: Every node contested by two or more operators' overlapping scope projections shall resolve to exactly one deterministic presenter.
requirements.why: M18/P-scope. presenterForNode (internal/invariants/scope_process.go) returns the LEXICOGRAPHICALLY FIRST operator id among the operators contesting a node -- a stable, total, deterministic rule that does not depend on graph declaration order (which shifts under unrelated source reformatting) or on an arbitrary parent-hierarchy walk. check_scoped_node_has_single_presenter (internal/invariants/scope_process.go) walks every pair of operators with a non-empty Scope, computes their overlap via projectScope, and fires a Violation only if presenterForNode ever returned empty for a non-empty contesting set -- which cannot happen, making single-presentership PROVABLE rather than merely asserted. Registered in the invariant registry (internal/invariants/all_violations.go) and in the methodology as Canon §Scope.

requirements.claim: Parallel agent invocations that mutate tracked repository files shall use git worktree isolation unless their target files are provably disjoint and no history-rewriting git operation is planned during their execution window.
requirements.why: Incident 2026-06-30/07-01: two parallel agents (P5-noise-fix and enforceability-flag tasks) both touched overlapping graph and invariants territory in one shared working tree. A subsequent git filter-repo hard-reset (run by the director to purge .idea/__pycache__ from committed history) wiped one agent's uncommitted work because it had not yet been committed and was not isolated in a worktree. The fix had to be redone from scratch. This requirement crystallizes the lesson: parallel mutating agents belong in isolated worktrees, or the director must guarantee no history-rewrite runs while their work is uncommitted. Not yet implemented as an enforced mechanism -- no spawn-log / worktree-isolation-status tooling exists (the spawn-log-isolation-status tool is Planned, not Implemented). The discipline is INHERENTLY_PROSE: it is a fact about an operating procedure (parallel agent orchestration), not a property of the committed graph that a check_* can verify. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Operator sub-domains shall relate to the parent graph by a single declared discipline (strict partition or declared-border overlap).
requirements.why: REJECTED -- REPLACES R-scope-is-projection + R-scope-overlap-generated + R-overlap-single-presenter. The open question ('strict partition, or overlap on explicitly-declared delegation borders?') presupposed a binary; the actual answer is a third option -- a PROJECTION. Operator sub-domains are neither a strict partition (which would forbid any shared node) nor an ad-hoc declared-border overlap (which would need hand-maintained edge lists); they are a computed, always-current id-set VIEW over the one shared graph (hotam_spec.scope_projection.project_scope), and any overlap between two operators' views is itself computed and rendered visibly (scope_overlap + the generator's OVERLAP block) rather than declared by hand or forbidden outright. A contested node under such an overlap resolves to exactly one deterministic presenter (R-overlap-single-presenter), closing the ambiguity the OPEN question was protecting against without adopting either extreme it named. -- (was: M18. Delegation (R-context-bounded-delegation) needs to know whether shared objects are forbidden (partition) or first-class borders; the two give different drift behavior.)

requirements.claim: Each graph node (Requirement/Conflict/Assumption/...) shall be persisted in its own separate JSON file, with a schema validating each file on load, instead of living in the single graph.py.
requirements.why: REJECTED — REPLACES by storage = the Python code itself. (a) Technically this is NOT R-rdf-store: no RDF triples, no SHACL shapes, no SPARQL — just one plain JSON file per node plus a load-time schema check. (b) It is rejected anyway because it violates the same underlying principle the RDF proposal violated: storage = the Python code itself. Per-node JSON would introduce a parallel source of truth (files that can drift from graph.py's build_graph()) and a schema layer duplicating the frozen-dataclass constructors — the exact anti-pattern criticized in SHACL (validation that re-states the check_* invariants / the constructors). The generated docs/gen/graph.json snapshot already gives any reader a machine-readable export, read-only, with the Python code remaining the single writer. (c) REVISIT WHEN either of two triggers appears: a SECOND legitimate writer of the graph emerges (something other than apply_proposal.py editing graph.py), OR a real non-Python consumer appears for which the generated export is insufficient and needs the store itself to be language-neutral. — (was: External product review proposed per-node JSON files as the primary store.)

requirements.claim: Proposals shall be organized under wave-numbered folders in `proposals/` (`waveN-<slug>/`, optionally with batch subfolders), which serves the pending-vs-applied practical need without a dedicated `pending/`/`applied/` runtime split or a new graph node type; a proposal file lives in its wave folder from the TRANSLATE step through LAND.
requirements.why: Steward verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «да, нужно. наверно такие вещи нужно вести в отдельной папке» (English: 'yes, it's needed. probably such things should be tracked in a separate folder'). UPDATED 2026-07-13 (task #113, decision brief §9): reworded from 'shall live in a dedicated runtime folder structure (pending/ vs applied/), surfaced by the harness' to legalize the OBSERVED practice — proposals live in wave-numbered folders under proposals/ (waveN-<slug>/, optionally with batch subfolders), which already serves the pending/applied practical need without a formal pre-approval staging step or a new graph node type. Verified today (2026-07-13): proposals/ carries 13 wave-named folders spanning wave1 through wave9 (9 wave numbers, several multi-folder: wave1×2, wave3×3, wave9×2), plus 2 task*-named folders = 15 entries total; wave6 alone landed 8 sub-batches in one session. The wave-folder practice models the 'applied' half well; the 'pending' half is handled in-workflow (TRANSLATE drafts a .json, PRESENT shows the steward, LAND applies) without needing an on-disk pre-approval artifact. The literal pending/applied split (option (a)) would require a workflow change — changing WHEN a proposal file gets written (before vs after approval) — which is more invasive than the engineering task alone (decision brief §9). The P6/PENDING_PROPOSAL label in cmd/hotam/what_now.go priorityLabel remains dead code (no signal producer); no .runtime/ proposal tracking exists. (Historical Python landing split spec/.runtime/proposals/ into pending/ vs applied/, moved by apply_proposal.py — not carried into the Go port.) PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists.

FOUND ISSUE (not fixed): enforcement is ENFORCED with a no-op enforcer that cannot verify the claim because it operates on the in-memory graph, not the filesystem. requirements.claim: internal/ontology/process.go shall define Process, Step, Goal, TargetState structs, plus ProcessLifecycle and GoalLifecycle canonical lifecycle variables.

All six types are defined in internal/ontology/process.go: Process (struct: ID, Lifecycle, Steps, RolesRequired, DrivesEntities, Why, DeclOrder), Step (struct: Name, RequiresRole, Invokes, Why), Goal (struct: ID, Owner, TargetState, Lifecycle, Why, DeclOrder), TargetState (struct: Kind, Predicate, Target), plus the canonical ProcessLifecycle (READY -> RUNNING -> DONE/ABANDONED) and GoalLifecycle (ACTIVE -> MET/ABANDONED) variables. requirements.claim: The project's name shall be Hotam-Spec (display) and hotam-spec (kebab-case for domain-level artifacts — domain directory names like `domains/hotam-spec-self`, the `hotam` CLI binary name, and repository slug conventions), while the Go module path shall match the real git remote and is deliberately `github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpec` (PascalCase suffix, per commit 4325ac8), closing M1.
requirements.why: M1 (package name) was OPEN since the framework's first incarnation as 'tensio'. The rename to Hotam-Spec aligns the project with its repository name and the user's chosen identity. UPDATED 2026-07-13 (task #113, decision brief §4): the Go module-path-suffix clause was reworded to legalize the current, deliberately-chosen module path `github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpec` (PascalCase suffix, matching the real git remote — set in commit 4325ac8 'chore: rename Go module to match the real git remote'). The earlier kebab-case-for-module-suffix demand was visibly drifted from reality: the actual go.mod reads 'module github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpec', and renaming it back to kebab-case would be a 119-file / 178-import-occurrence mechanical change that reverses a conscious recent decision (decision brief §4 option (b) over (a), which was flagged as the highest-risk option in the whole batch of 9). Kebab-case remains the rule for domain-level artifacts that already legitimately are kebab/lowercase (directory names like domains/hotam-spec-self, the hotam CLI binary name) — only the module-path-suffix clause changed to match reality. Convention: 'Hotam-Spec' in prose (capitalized hyphen). (Historically a Python package 'hotam_spec' with a PyPI 'hotam-spec' distribution; the project is now a Go module.) Renames completed in three sequential passes (#89 package, #90 domain, #91 prose).

requirements.claim: HotamSpec resolves the consumer's project root through a single function, paths.ProjectRoot() (internal/paths/project_root.go), via a documented R1-R6 priority chain, never through a raw runtime.Caller-based parents-climb guess at the consumer's files.
requirements.why: paths.ProjectRoot() (internal/paths/project_root.go) resolves the project root via a six-level priority chain: R1 env HOTAM_SPEC_PROJECT_ROOT, R2 env HOTAM_SPEC_DOMAINS_ROOT (parent), R3 bottom-up search for Go-native markers (domains/, delegations/, or the SECONDARY-marker quorum), R4 .hotam-spec-project marker file, R5 legacy pyproject.toml [tool.hotam-spec].project_root fallback, R6 self-hosting fallback (repo root via go.mod). Every path that reads/writes consumer content (domains/, tickets/, CLAUDE.md, docs/gen) is derived from this resolver. FOUND ISSUE (not fixed): the historical Python claim asserted an AST-scanning enforcer (test_project_root_not_hardcoded.py) that syntactically scans every committed framework .py for a Path(__file__).resolve().parents[N] climb joined to consumer data. No such AST scanner exists -- there is no Go equivalent that syntactically scans internal/*.go for a runtime.Caller-based parents-climb to consumer data. The named enforcer TestProjectRoot_Priority_NativeMarkerBeatsPyproject (internal/paths/) tests the resolver's R3-over-R5 priority, NOT the 'no hardcoded consumer-root climb' discipline. The enforcement/enforced_by are out of scope for this content batch; enforcement is ENFORCED with a priority-chain test that does not verify the no-hardcode claim. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: The proposal protocol shall include a ProposedConflict kind (kind='Conflict') that materializes a new Conflict node in the active domain's graph via `hotam apply-proposal`.
requirements.why: Closes recorded spec-tool drift: a DECIDED rationale already promised 'ProposedConflict' while only transitions existed, leaving surfaced tensions without a mechanical creation path (the loop could move conflicts but never materialize one). ProposedConflict (internal/proposal/types.go) is a real proposal kind: ProposedConflict.mutate (internal/proposal/mutate.go) computes the id via ontology.ConflictIdentity(axis, context), never caller-supplied (R-stable-conflict-identity); validate() (internal/proposal/validate.go) requires the axis to already exist in the graph's axes (R-axis-controlled-vocab; admitting a new axis is a separate act), requires >= 2 distinct member requirement ids (R-conflict-min-two-members), and refuses a steward who owns any member (R-steward-distinct-from-owners); lifecycle always starts DETECTED (or a caller-supplied initial_lifecycle, which must carry a decided_by if DECIDED). Applied via `hotam apply-proposal` (cmd/hotam/apply_proposal.go) or `hotam land`. Enforced by TestApply_Conflict_Create (internal/proposal/proposal_test.go). Extends R-active-loop-protocol's floor exactly as EntityType and OperatorBudget did. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.
