requirements.claim: The proposal protocol shall include a ProposedStakeholder kind (Kind="Stakeholder") that materializes a new Stakeholder node in the active domain's graph by appending to g.Stakeholders, refusing a duplicate id.
requirements.why: Wave 13 (the stranger's door). A newcomer who clones the repo for their own business domain is locked out at the first Conflict: check_steward_not_a_member_owner requires a steward who is NOT the owner of any member Requirement, i.e. at least two distinct Stakeholders must exist before any tension can be held. Yet every Requirement, Axis and Assumption already had a Proposed* door while Stakeholder did not, trapping the newcomer between R-no-hand-edit-graph (the graph is writable only through proposals) and the absence of a door. ProposedStakeholder is that door: a struct in internal/proposal/types.go (KindStakeholder="Stakeholder", fields ID/Name/Domain/Why) whose mutate method (internal/proposal/mutate.go: ProposedStakeholder.mutate) checks ontology.StakeholderIDs(g) for the exact id and, if absent, appends an ontology.Stakeholder{ID,Name,Domain} to g.Stakeholders. It models the exact ProposedAxis/ProposedAssumption pattern (append-to-slice, exact-id-uniqueness); extends R-active-loop-protocol's floor exactly as EntityType, OperatorBudget, Axis and Assumption did. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: The operator's self-diagnosis conditions shall be named, pure, graph-only predicate functions in internal/diagnose/finding.go composed by AllFindings in a fixed registry order, and consumed by the what-now harness via internal/diagnose/signal.go -- never inlined in tool code.
requirements.why: Concept-Map gap closed: §Reflection is the home in internal/ for the P0 self-diagnosis conditions. These live as eight named predicates in internal/diagnose/finding.go -- ReflectDraftOverhang, ReflectUnenforcedSettled, ReflectOverBudgetOperators, ReflectDeadAssumptionOnEnforcer, ReflectDerivedButUnbuilt, ReflectImplementsDecay, ReflectReplacesEdgeMigration, ReflectAllMembersRejected -- each returning []Finding (Condition/Target/Imperative records). The registry order is fixed by AllFindings(g), which appends each predicate's findings in that exact sequence (no per-call reordering); the what-now harness consumes them through internal/diagnose/signal.go (collectAttentionSignals ranges over AllFindings(g)) and renders, exactly as it composes invariants.AllViolations -- the substrate owns the diagnosis vocabulary, the tool only renders. This is the same extraction pattern as the §Invariants layer: reflect_* predicates return Finding records and the harness composes AllFindings() the way it composes AllViolations(). TestAllFindings_OrderFollowsRegistry (internal/diagnose/finding_test.go) pins the registry order. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: Each Requirement.claim shall assert exactly one concern, with conjunctions of distinct concerns decomposed into separate requirements.
requirements.why: Not yet implemented: no mechanical enforcer exists. The historical mechanical enforcer was tools/audit_atomicity.py (surfacing Requirements with compound claims as a deterministic audit signal, per docs/gen/AUDIT.md) plus tests/test_atomicity_ratchet.py::test_no_new_compound_requirements_beyond_baseline as a strict zero-COMPOUND gate over live SETTLED/OPEN claims, with an atomicity_compound_baseline.json ratchet. audit-atomicity is Planned (not implemented) in the methodology.Tools registry, and no check_* or test enforces claim atomicity -- the discipline is currently carried by review convention only. The substance (one concern per claim, compound claims split into separate atoms) remains the live editorial discipline; only its mechanical detection is not yet implemented. R-atomicity-ratchet-no-growth is the sibling requirement meant to carry the growth-direction half; both are PROSE (not ENFORCED). Waves 1-3 of atomization applied the discipline to the meta-domain manually; this content refresh itself follows that same hand-applied atomicity discipline. RECLASSIFIED 2026-07-13: enforceability ENFORCEABLE -> INHERENTLY_PROSE (agent consultation, fm) -- whether a claim asserts 'exactly one concern' is a semantic judgment (no parser reliably distinguishes a conjunction of distinct concerns from a compound description of one concern); a heuristic classifier is at best advisory, not a proof of atomicity. The mechanical, provable half of this discipline (growth-direction ratchet against a baseline) is carried by the sibling R-atomicity-ratchet-no-growth, which remains ENFORCEABLE. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.

requirements.claim: A Requirement carries optional freshness fields (last_reviewed_at, review_after, evidence, source_refs) and a DERIVED, append-only per-node change history (history: slice of HistoryEntry), where each HistoryEntry is written by the proposal system from the field diff on every UPDATE of an already-existing node (never at first creation, never hand-authored), and the history trail is STRUCTURALLY well-formed: every entry has a non-empty at-stamp and summary, and stamps are monotonically non-decreasing.
requirements.why: External product review (2026-07-10) proposed four freshness fields (last_reviewed_at/review_after/evidence/source_refs) plus recording each node's change history IN the committed graph.json -- not only in git blame (which tracks source lines, not semantic field transitions) and not only in gitignored .runtime JSON (ephemeral, R-task-spawn-log-runtime). Steward accepted all four fields + in-file per-node history. The freshness fields live on ontology.Requirement (internal/ontology/requirement.go) and its History []HistoryEntry; the proposal writer (ProposedRequirement.mutate in internal/proposal/mutate.go) computes the old->new field diff via summarizeFieldDiff and, on UPDATE of an existing node only (never at CREATE), appends an ontology.HistoryEntry{At: today, Summary: diff} -- mirroring the append-only ticket History form so the two change-trails do not diverge. The syntactic enforcer is check_requirement_history_wellformed (internal/invariants/lifecycle_checks.go): three sub-rules over each Requirement's history -- non-empty `at`, non-empty `summary`, monotonically non-decreasing `at` (ISO-8601 sorts lexicographically == chronologically). The check is deliberately SYNTACTIC ONLY: measuring the SUBSTANCE of a freshness record would repeat the form-metric theatre that sank R-boot-cite-measured (REJECTED). CLOSED LOOPHOLE (2026-07-13, task #101): this requirement's own last_reviewed_at had been stamped via a routine ProposedRequirement content-editing UPDATE with evidence:[] -- the UPDATE path (internal/proposal/mutate.go, ProposedRequirement.mutate) coalesced last_reviewed_at/review_after with zero evidence gate, letting a freshness stamp ride silently alongside an unrelated content edit in one history entry, defeating the whole point of R-review-mark-carries-evidence. Fixed in ProposedRequirement.validate() (internal/proposal/validate.go): setting last_reviewed_at or review_after via ANY ProposedRequirement (CREATE or UPDATE) now requires non-empty evidence, the identical rule ProposedReviewMark already enforced -- freshness fields are evidence-gated regardless of entry point. Content-only edits that never touch either freshness field are unaffected (coalesceStr's empty-preserves-existing default still applies with zero evidence required).
evidence: cd D:/ai_dev/prat/HotamSpec && go test ./internal/proposal/... -run TestApply_Requirement_UpdateAppendsHistory -v : PASS (task #226 recursion, 2026-07-16); HistoryEntry (internal/ontology/requirement.go) is appended by ProposedRequirement.mutate (internal/proposal/mutate.go) via summarizeFieldDiff on UPDATE only, exercised end-to-end by TestApply_Requirement_UpdateAppendsHistory

requirements.claim: A ProposedReviewMark proposal shall fail validation unless it carries at least one non-whitespace evidence entry.
requirements.why: Structural well-formedness gate, not a substance-quality metric: it asserts only that the attestation artifact EXISTS (at least one non-blank evidence string), never that the review was truthful. This keeps it clear of R-boot-cite-measured (REJECTED, REPLACES by R-agent-conduct-is-rules-not-tests), which was a lexical form-metric that produced a compliance score nobody read. This gate produces no metric and no log; it is a fail-loud validation precondition, the same class of syntactic-only enforcement already blessed by R-requirement-freshness-fields / check_requirement_history_wellformed (which requires non-empty at/summary on History entries — the same 'record must not be blank' principle). The substance of the evidence IS reviewed, but by the STEWARD at the present moment per R-review-mark-requires-substantive-review (INHERENTLY_PROSE); this change is a structural PRECONDITION of that existing PROSE discipline — it guarantees evidence exists to be reviewed — not a replacement or duplicate. Without it, a ReviewMark can stamp last_reviewed_at with zero evidence, indistinguishable from the cmd/seed-due administrative date backfill whose stale attestations the P0-4 content-refresh wave later had to repair. Forward-looking only: the rule constrains the PROPOSAL, not the graph — existing SETTLED nodes that legitimately carry empty evidence remain valid graph state after this change; the gate bites on the next ReviewMark applied. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestApply_ReviewMark_WithoutEvidenceFails and TestApply_ReviewMark_WhitespaceEvidenceFails in internal/proposal/review_mark_test.go mechanically verify a proposal with reviewed_at/review_after set but empty or whitespace-only evidence is rejected by validation.
evidence: Re-ran `go test -run 'TestApply_ReviewMark_WithoutEvidenceFails|TestApply_ReviewMark_WhitespaceEvidenceFails' ./internal/proposal/...` on 2026-07-13: both pass — `ok github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpec/internal/proposal 1.385s` — confirming the two named enforcers in internal/proposal/review_mark_test.go actually reject an evidence-less and a whitespace-only-evidence ProposedReviewMark respectively, exactly as the requirement's enforced_by claims. File and test names verified by reading internal/proposal/review_mark_test.go directly before citing them, not guessed.

Live self-demonstration: this very review-mark proposal (this file) could not be constructed as a valid ProposedReviewMark without a populated, non-whitespace evidence array — internal/proposal/validate.go's ProposedReviewMark.validate() (lines 299-311) unconditionally requires len(trimNonEmpty(p.Evidence)) > 0, so the act of landing this mark is itself a live, reproducible exercise of the rule it attests to, not merely a claim about past behavior.

The requirement's own why field states it is a structural well-formedness gate (existence of an attestation, not a truthfulness check) — the two cited tests match that scope exactly: TestApply_ReviewMark_WithoutEvidenceFails covers the missing-evidence case, TestApply_ReviewMark_WhitespaceEvidenceFails covers the present-but-blank case (via trimNonEmpty), leaving no gap between the claim and what is mechanically checked.

requirements.claim: A SETTLED requirement's last_reviewed_at shall be updated only after its claim/why/evidence/source_refs have actually been read and checked against current code/graph reality; a blanket date backfill with no substantive check is not a review and must not set last_reviewed_at.
requirements.why: This session's own history is the cautionary precedent. cmd/seed-due (removed, P2-2) backfilled last_reviewed_at/review_after on every SETTLED requirement from settled_at/created_at with no actual re-read of the text -- necessary to bootstrap the freshness engine (R-requirement-freshness-fields) with real dates instead of a universal NEVER-REVIEWED, but a review in name only: at the moment of backfill, 155+ of those requirements still described the superseded implementation (the P0-4 content-refresh wave that followed found and fixed exactly that staleness). A freshness field that can be set without reading the content it claims to attest defeats its own purpose -- hotam due would report those 155+ requirements as fresh while they were substantively wrong. This rule closes that gap going forward: a genuine review means confronting the current claim/why/evidence/source_refs against the real code or graph state (the same discipline the mediation loop's CONFRONT step already names) before touching last_reviewed_at, not merely rolling a date forward. Structurally unenforceable by design -- no mechanical check can verify a human or agent actually read and understood text, the same honesty boundary that sank R-boot-cite-measured (REJECTED, form-metric theatre) -- so this stays PROSE discipline, upheld by steward review of what a ProposedReviewMark or ProposedRequirement UPDATE's evidence/why actually says, not by an automated check. RECLASSIFIED 2026-07-13: enforceability ENFORCEABLE -> INHERENTLY_PROSE (agent consultation, fm) -- this requirement's own text already argued unenforceability by design; the enforceability field is now brought in line with that argument, removing it from the closeable-debt count honestly rather than leaving it counted as fixable-but-unfixed.

requirements.claim: The what-now harness shall surface a CLI-only action for each DECIDED conflict whose revisit_marker has never been evaluated or was last evaluated more than the staleness delta of SETTLED atoms ago.
requirements.why: Not yet implemented: a revisit-evaluation band does not exist. A DECIDED conflict's revisit_marker names the CONDITION under which the decision should be re-opened (the historian's anti-relitigation trigger), but nothing tracked whether anyone LOOKED at it again -- an unread trigger lets the decision silently ossify while its stated revisit condition may already have come true. The mark-revisit-evaluated writer is Planned (not implemented) in methodology.Tools, and the what-now harness (cmd/hotam/what_now.go over internal/diagnose/signal.go) renders only graph-derived signals -- there is no revisit-evaluation band reading the filesystem (stays OUT of the signal set for the same determinism reason as the pending-proposal / generative-audit bands: it reads the filesystem, which gen-spec must not render). (Historical note: the original implementation was tools/mark_revisit_evaluated.py, which appended a {stamp, conflict, settled_count} record to spec/.runtime/revisit-eval.jsonl when the steward evaluated a marker, and tools/what_now.py re-surfaced the marker when never evaluated or stale by more than GENERATIVE_AUDIT_STALE_DELTA SETTLED atoms.) The conflict.RevisitMarker field itself is carried and written by ProposedConflictTransition.mutate, so the marker survives in substrate; only the evaluation-tracking reader/writer is unimplemented. Evaluating a marker is an OBSERVATION; re-opening the conflict is a separate ProposedConflictTransition the steward drafts (R-ai-presents-not-decides). PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists.

requirements.claim: Root CLAUDE.md shall embed the full content of the active domain's CLAUDE.md inside a DOMAIN-CRYSTAL sentinel block generated by gen_spec.py.
requirements.why: REJECTED — REPLACES R-claude-md-consolidates-when-single-agent. This claim described embedding a SEPARATE domains/hotam-spec-self/CLAUDE.md inside root CLAUDE.md via a DOMAIN-CRYSTAL sentinel block. That separate file no longer exists (deleted in task #101's consolidation) — there is nothing left to embed, since the domain content is generated directly into the single root CLAUDE.md rather than composed from a nested crystal. The two-file DOMAIN-CRYSTAL composition pattern returns only when a second domain is created and root CLAUDE.md can no longer hold all domains inline. — (was: Closes the sensor-substrate gap: Claude Code auto-loads root CLAUDE.md on session start; embedding the domain's CLAUDE.md (the canonical entry point of the domain, and the base from which all sub-agents derive scoped versions) means the operator boots from substrate (R-operator-prompt-from-substrate) rather than from raw weights + session memory. The substrate writes the operator's prompt physically, not aspirationally.)

requirements.claim: The root CLAUDE.md (and its AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md siblings) shall be rendered by gen-spec from a template whose body is a minimal framework-identity header plus sentinel-bounded generated blocks, with no hand-written prose between sentinels.
requirements.why: Substrate-generates-operator at root level. Hand-written prose drifts; the framework's mind is assembled by gen-spec from spec/docs/thinking/* (shared DRY source) and the domain graph, and the root is a thin shell pointing into both. The root CLAUDE.md is produced by generator.RenderClaudeMDFromTemplate (internal/generator/claudemd.go) when gen-spec is invoked with --claude-md pointing at the root path; the same bytes are also written to AGENTS.md and GEMINI.md (cmd/hotam/gen_spec.go). The body uses the sentinel-pair pattern from internal/generator/claudemd_sentinel.go (WrapBlock/ExtractBlock), so every generated region sits between e.g. <!-- LIVE-STATE:BEGIN --> ... <!-- LIVE-STATE:END --> markers and is rewritten wholesale on each regen, making hand-edits between sentinels structurally impossible to preserve. RESOLVED -- REPLACES the old hand-written prose in root CLAUDE.md (P19a). FOUND ISSUE (not fixed): no test or check_* verifies the on-disk root CLAUDE.md contains no hand-written prose between sentinels (the original structural enforcer, tests/test_root_claude_md_is_sentinel_only.py, was not carried over). The property holds today because the file is regenerated wholesale from the template, but the guard against a future hand-edit is absent; the requirement is STRUCTURAL with no enforced_by. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestRenderClaudeMDFromTemplate_NoProseBetweenSentinels in internal/generator/claudemd_coverage_test.go mechanically verifies this claim against the generated crystal on the fixture graph.

requirements.claim: Regular invariant families (homogeneous per-entity structural checks such as dangling-refs, typed-anchors, and lifecycle-membership) shall be CLASSIFIED as table-driven data distinct from irreducibly bespoke invariants (identity derivation, cross-entity bijections, docstring/body coherence, budget arithmetic) -- with derivation of check_* function bodies from any such table explicitly deferred.
requirements.why: M22, decided 2026-07-02 by steward verdict HYBRID (criterion: whatever is most convenient for the agents). Landed reality: there is no RULES_AS_DATA_TABLE metadata data structure -- the classification lives implicitly in the code structure (table-driven checks iterate g.EntityTypes or similar collections; bespoke checks walk specific graph topology), so there is no metadata table for an agent to read. check_rules_as_data_classification_coherent IS registered in the invariant registry (internal/invariants/self_reference.go) but is an HONEST NO-OP -- checkRulesAsDataClassificationCoherent returns nil unconditionally, because without a RULES_AS_DATA_TABLE there is nothing to drift-check against the registry; its Why field states this explicitly and notes that if a future milestone reintroduces explicit classification metadata the check should be revived. The body-derivation question is moot (there is no table to derive from). (Historical note: the original implementation carried a RULES_AS_DATA_TABLE with 31 TABLE_DRIVEN / 32 BESPOKE rows and deferred deriving check_* bodies from it behind an inspect.getsource blocker.) FOUND ISSUE (not fixed): this requirement is ENFORCED with check_rules_as_data_classification_coherent, but that enforcer is a structural no-op -- it cannot detect any violation because the classification table it would guard does not exist. The enforcement/enforced_by are out of scope for this content batch; documented as debt rather than reworded into a lie.

requirements.claim: An operator's sub-domain shall be a computed PROJECTION (an id-set view derived by prefix match over the shared graph), never a copy of any node.
requirements.why: M18/P-scope. Resolves R-partition-vs-border's open question by rejecting both extremes: a strict partition forbids any shared object (loses the ability to model deliberate cross-cutting delegation); an ad-hoc declared-border overlap invites hand-maintained edge lists that drift from the graph. The projection is projectScope(g, prefixes) in internal/invariants/scope_process.go: it builds a scopeView (sets of requirement+conflict ids) purely from the graph by id-prefix match (strings.HasPrefix(r.ID, p) for any p in the operator's Scope prefixes), on demand, with no copied node data. This is the exact prefix-match discipline the generator already uses for per-domain CONSTITUTION digests, so the projection can never fork from the single writer (R-no-hand-edit-graph). The declaring prefix tuple is carried by Operator.Scope (internal/ontology/operator.go). The projection is consumed by check_scoped_node_has_single_presenter to compute overlaps, so the projection is load-bearing in the invariant layer today even though only one OP-director (Scope=()) currently exists. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestProjectScopeIsIDProjectionNotCopy in internal/invariants/scope_process_test.go mechanically verifies this claim.

requirements.claim: When two operators' scope projections share a node, the overlap shall be computed (never hidden or silently merged); the rendering of that overlap into a per-operator crystal is deferred until a real second operator exists.
requirements.why: M18/P-scope. The overlap is computed by scopeOverlapNodeIDs(a, b scopeView) in internal/invariants/scope_process.go -- the sorted set-intersection of two scopeViews over requirement+conflict ids -- and is consumed by check_scoped_node_has_single_presenter, which walks every unordered pair of operators with a non-empty Scope, projects each, and fires only if a contested node has no determinable presenter. So the COMPUTE half of the claim is live and load-bearing in the invariant layer. The RENDER half -- emitting one OVERLAP:BEGIN/END block per agent against every OTHER discovered agent's SCOPE -- is not implemented: the generator (internal/generator) produces a single consolidated root CLAUDE.md and has no per-agent crystal generation at all, because with the current meta-domain (one OP-director, SCOPE=()) there are no agents to render overlap for (R-claude-md-consolidates-when-single-agent). (Historical note: the original render was tools/gen_spec.py::_regenerate_agent_constitutions, which emitted one OVERLAP block per agent.) The glossary still defines §Scope as 'overlaps with another operator's projection are computed and rendered, never hidden' (internal/generator/glossary_terms_data.go); the compute is real, the per-agent render is deferred, not abandoned -- it materializes when R-sub-agent-crystal-triad's per-agent generation is implemented alongside a real second operator. requirements.claim: Each docs/gen/tools/<command>.md shall be a pure function of that tool's methodology.Tool registry entry (Command/Canon/Purpose/Status), generated wholesale by BuildToolDocs with no hand-written content.
requirements.why: A tool doc that diverges from the tool's real declaration is worse than no doc -- it misleads operators. Each tool doc is generated by generator.BuildToolDocs() (internal/generator/tooldocs.go): for every tool in methodology.Tools it renders a single document from the registry fields only -- a status-aware H1 (statusBadge: [IMPLEMENTED]/[PLANNED -- not implemented]), a Status section (statusLine), the Canon section token, and the Purpose line -- and writes it to domains/<domain>/docs/gen/tools/<command>.md via gen-spec (cmd/hotam/gen_spec.go). The whole file is regenerated wholesale on each gen-spec run (a single AUTOGENERATED banner at the top), so there are no inner sentinels and the doc literally cannot diverge from the registry entry that defines the tool. (Historical note: the original mechanism derived two sections from the module docstring + argparse --help output between hand-edit sentinels; that AST-harvest does not apply here.) The single-source discipline is preserved and strengthened. TestBuildToolDocsSemanticCompleteness (internal/generator/thinkingdocs_tooldocs_test.go) verifies every methodology.Tools entry yields a non-empty doc containing its Canon, Purpose, and Command.

requirements.claim: A steward signoff on a DECIDED/HELD Conflict or a transitioned Assumption shall be preserved as a Signoff payload IN the graph node (not only in gitignored proposal JSON) -- decided_by, date, verbatim (optional), instrument and chosen_variant (for HELD->DECIDED) are auditable from the substrate.
requirements.why: K2(a) from lens-1-ontology review: AssumptionTransition's decided_by was required at validation but the writer wrote only status+reason to the graph, so the signature evaporated into gitignored JSON -- R-trust-anchor-mechanism was not auditable from the substrate. K2(b): HELD->DECIDED erased Variants (the non-chosen implies/costs -- anti-relitigation cargo -- were overwritten); the chosen variant lived only as prose inside DECIDED(...). The Signoff payload is ontology.Signoff (internal/ontology/signoff.go): a struct (NOT a new node type, anti-RDF -- mirrors Variant's rationale) carrying DecidedBy + Date + Verbatim + Instrument + ChosenVariant, attached as *Signoff on Conflict.Signoff and Assumption.Signoff (internal/ontology/conflict.go, internal/ontology/assumption.go). The proposal writer builds it: ProposedConflictTransition.mutate (internal/proposal/mutate.go) sets c.Signoff = &ontology.Signoff{...} when the transition is a decision with a non-empty DecidedBy (Instrument defaults to SignoffInstrumentPersonal), and ProposedAssumptionTransition.mutate does the equivalent for assumptions; variants are preserved across HELD->DECIDED. Instrument (SignoffInstrumentPersonal / SignoffInstrumentDEL, internal/ontology/signoff.go) is an explicit seam for the future verifiable-signature wave (R-decided-by-verifiable-signature is OPEN -- this atom does NOT bind to git/crypto, only names the seam). check_signoff_chosen_variant_resolves (internal/invariants/typed_anchors.go) enforces a non-empty chosen_variant is the id of one of the conflict's variants; check_decided_conflict_carries_signoff enforces consistency (signoff.decided_by == conflict.decided_by) when a signoff is present, SOFT (pre-existing decisions without signoff are legitimate, no migration forcing).

requirements.claim: The test suite shall provide a fast end-to-end signal that the framework is healthy on the real domain graph -- load the graph, run every generator template, and regenerate docs without panicking or empty output.
requirements.why: An agent after a change should not need to remember the full test count or layout -- one signal means 'the framework did not fall over on real data'. A distributed set of tests together covers that ground: TestGenSpec_SmokeOnRealDomain (internal/generator/byteidentical_test.go) loads the real hotam-spec-self domain graph (domains/hotam-spec-self/graph.json) and runs every generator Build* template (REQUIREMENTS/TENSIONS/OPEN/UNENFORCED/GLOSSARY/HISTORY/DECISIONS/CONSTITUTION/ENTITIES/FRAMEWORK-INVARIANTS/REPO-MAP/live-state plus BuildGraphJSON), asserting non-empty output and no panic; the all-violations coverage is exercised by internal/invariants' own real-graph tests; the harness path is exercised by internal/diagnose integration tests (TestAllFindings_RealGraphNoPanic) and the cmd/hotam CLI tests. The single-test shape is split across packages because tests run per-package, but the collective signal is the same one-health-check intent. (Historical note: this role was originally one end-to-end test, spec/tests/test_smoke.py.) PROSE (not ENFORCED): no single check_* gates this; it is the test suite's role, not an invariant. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestSmoke_EveryBuildTemplateOnRealDomainNoPanicNoEmpty in internal/generator/byteidentical_test.go mechanically verifies this claim.

requirements.claim: An operator shall communicate by reference, ensuring every assertion cites at least one concrete anchor in the info-space.
requirements.why: SETTLED (P5): the references-not-content discipline is now structurally bound. The structural half is enforced by check_section_anchors_known (internal/invariants/self_reference.go): it ranges over every registered invariant in the All registry and fires a STRUCTURE violation whenever an invariant's Canon is nil -- i.e. an invariant that fails to reference a registered methodology.Section. Every Invariant.Canon is a TYPED *methodology.Section pointer obtained at registration time from methodology.Sections.MustRegister -- a typed pointer into the registry cannot dangle, so the only remaining failure mode (a nil Canon) is what this check catches directly. (Historical note: the original mechanism walked every source file with ast, extracted section-sign tokens from docstrings via regex, and checked each against the glossary; that AST+regex walk is structurally unnecessary once the Canon is a typed pointer.) The §Glossary itself lives as DATA in methodology.Sections (internal/methodology/sections_data.go), so an invented §-token cannot compile. The advisory what-now/inspect output cites anchor ids in every action (the Finding/Signal Target fields). Together these make reference-not-content structurally visible and machine-checked. (Wave 1 seed-coherence pass: enforced_by entries that named bare doc paths or since-renamed tests were corrected to the enforcer that actually covers this claim, under the check_enforced_by_resolvable discipline.)

requirements.claim: An authored-spec requirement's link to code shall carry two ORTHOGONAL fields, never collapsed into one: implemented_by names WHERE the requirement is EMBODIED (a path-qualified file:symbol reference into the domain's authored spec/ tree, the model/method that realizes the claim), and verified_by names WHERE the requirement is PROVEN (a path-qualified file:test reference, the executable test that demonstrates the claim holds). A symbol that embodies a requirement without a test that proves it, or a test cited without the symbol it exercises, is an incomplete SpecLink -- ENFORCED status requires both halves together (see R-enforced-requires-enforcer-or-authored-link), but the two fields answer two DIFFERENT questions and shall never be merged into a single field or treated as aliases of one another.
requirements.why: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §4 ("Схема связи: requirement -> code -> test") and §12 (steward decision 2026-07-16) establish implemented_by/verified_by as two separate fields on Requirement (internal/ontology/requirement.go), landed additively in task #223 and already carrying inline doc comments naming the same embodied/proven distinction (requirement.go:80-96: "ImplementedBy names WHERE this requirement is embodied", "VerifiedBy names WHERE this requirement is PROVEN"). R-authored-spec-links-mechanically-checked (#223) and R-enforced-requires-enforcer-or-authored-link (#223) already model the MECHANICAL checking of these fields (symbol/test resolvability, non-vacuousness, no-skip, no-reuse) and the DISJUNCTIVE ENFORCED gate that accepts either enforced_by or implemented_by+verified_by together -- but neither requirement states the CONCEPTUAL distinction itself: why two fields exist, what each one MEANS, and why they are not interchangeable. Without this requirement as a first-class node, the SpecLink concept (PLAN §4's requirement->code->test schema) exists only in code comments and PLAN prose, not as a graph anchor a future agent can cite when modeling a business domain's own spec/ tree (task #224 pilot, task #236 recursion into hotam-spec-self's own engine code). This requirement is the missing OBJECT-level node: it names the SpecLink concept and its two fields' distinct semantics, one layer above the two already-landed mechanical-check requirements which govern HOW the fields are validated, not WHAT they mean. PROSE/SETTLED is correct here: this is a naming-and-definition claim about a data shape already implemented (internal/ontology/requirement.go ImplementedBy/VerifiedBy, task #223) -- the fields themselves are code, but THIS requirement's own implemented_by/verified_by linkage into hotam-spec-self's engine is deliberately deferred to task #236 (recursion), per the session's explicit instruction not to add code links at the objects+fields step.

requirements.claim: The Entity aspect, multi-domain federation, and sub-agent recursion machinery shall receive no inward development while frozen, unfreezing only when a real business domain demonstrates concrete need.
requirements.why: Built ahead of demand -- 0 entity_types/entities, exactly 1 domain, 0 active sub-agents against the supporting machinery: classic speculative generality. Frozen by steward 2026-07-02 after audit. The frozen surface is carried as Planned (not-implemented) methodology.Tools entries -- create-agent, spawn-agent, invoke-agent, create-domain (internal/methodology/tools_data.go) -- plus the Entity aspect substrate that exists but is inert (internal/ontology/entity.go, internal/invariants/scope_process.go Process/Entity checks that no-op when g.EntityTypes is empty). The natural home for this freeze is C-8600b1b8 (core-vs-aspect, ACKNOWLEDGED, revisit_marker reads 'REVISIT when a second opt-in behavioral aspect is proposed'); the freeze remains open pending steward decision on when to formally move C-8600b1b8 to DECIDED, and do not confuse 'now addressable via ConflictTransition proposals' with 'already resolved.' FOUND ISSUE (not fixed): there is no frozen-aspects snapshot guard -- an inward edit to a frozen file (e.g. adding entity-machinery behavior) would not fail a test. (Historical note: the original Wave 1 guard was a sha256 hash-baseline test, tests/frozen_aspects_baseline.json + test_frozen_aspects_snapshot.py, that made an inward edit fail RED; it was not carried over.) The freeze is currently carried by review discipline + the Planned tool status, not by a structural guard; the requirement is PROSE (not ENFORCED). Unfreeze trigger remains Phase 5 (a real business domain).

requirements.claim: Crystallized knowledge whose enforcing assumption has died shall be surfaced as stale (enforced-but-wrong, a bad habit).
requirements.why: SETTLED (P6): the structural detection path now exists. The stale-substrate signal is produced by ReflectDeadAssumptionOnEnforcer (internal/diagnose/finding.go): for each SETTLED+ENFORCED Requirement whose Assumptions include a DEAD one, it emits a Finding (Condition="reflect_dead_assumption_on_enforcer", Target=requirement id) whose Imperative names the dead assumption and the requirement resting on it -- surfacing the bad-habit case where an enforced rule's premise has quietly died. This Finding flows through AllFindings into the what-now attention signals (internal/diagnose/signal.go) via the same registry as every other reflection predicate (R-reflection-predicates-first-class). Today the hotam-spec-self domain carries zero DEAD assumptions, so no stale substrate actually fires; the structural detection path is in place for when one does. TestReflectDeadAssumptionOnEnforcer_Fires (internal/diagnose/finding_test.go) pins the firing path; TestReflectDeadAssumptionOnEnforcer_ProseNotEnforced pins that PROSE requirements do not trigger it.

requirements.claim: The hotam CLI shall provide a `status` command (`hotam status [--domain <path>] [--today YYYY-MM-DD] [--json]`) that composes what-now's top action + debt counts (internal/diagnose), due's freshness counts (internal/freshness), and all-violations' violation count (internal/invariants) into a single compact summary, so an agent does not need to run those three commands separately to reconstruct the same picture. It reuses the same underlying functions those commands call rather than reimplementing their logic, never gates (exit code always 0), and its --json output uses flat, explicitly named fields (top_action, settled_count, enforced_count, closeable_debt_count, overdue_count, never_reviewed_count, violation_count, node_count) parseable in one shot.
requirements.why: External review (TaskList P2 item #80) observed that an agent orienting itself has to run `what-now`, `due`, and `all-violations` separately and mentally merge the results, burning context on three graph loads and three renderings to reconstruct one picture. This mirrors the existing three-cipher pulse the root CLAUDE.md's LIVE-STATE block already carries (R-three-cipher-pulse-structurally-injected) but as an ad hoc, freshly-runnable CLI command rather than a static crystal block — useful mid-session when the crystal is stale or when a different domain than the resident one needs a quick pulse. Composition, not duplication: buildStatusReport (cmd/hotam/status.go) calls diagnose.DiagnoseSignals (the same call formatSignals/BuildLiveState make for what-now's top action and the LIVE-STATE debt line), freshness.ClassifyGraph (the same call buildDueReport makes), and invariants.AllViolations (the same call cmdAllViolations makes) on one already-loaded graph — it introduces no new definition of 'closeable debt', 'overdue', or 'violation'. Human-readable output is a handful of labeled lines (top action / debt / freshness / violations / graph), not a full report, matching the point of the command: a single-shot, low-context pulse. Exit code is always 0 like due/inspect/confront — status is advisory, not a gate; all-violations remains the hard gate command for invariant violations. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestStatusToolRegisteredImplemented (internal/methodology/methodology_test.go) enforces the existence half — the `status` tool is registered Implemented with a real Purpose, so the registry entry read by cmd/hotam/tool_wiring.go and the generated EMBEDDED-TOOLS crystal block can never silently regress. The behavioral half — buildStatusReport's aggregated numbers actually agreeing with what-now/due/all-violations — is proven by TestBuildStatusReport_MatchesWhatNowDueAllViolations (cmd/hotam/status_test.go), which independently recomputes every field via diagnose.DiagnoseSignals / freshness.ClassifyGraph / invariants.AllViolations on the same fixture graph; it cannot be named in enforced_by directly because internal/gate's Test*-name resolver (check_enforced_by_resolvable) is scoped to internal/**/*_test.go and cmd/hotam tests use unexported cmd/hotam helpers unreachable from internal/, but it is the test that actually guards against drift and is cited here for the audit trail. UPDATE 2026-07-13 (TaskList P2 item #81, external review): 'parseable in one shot' is refined to also mean array-typed fields are never JSON `null` for the empty case — a nil Go slice (`var x []T`) and an omitted/`[]` field are semantically identical ('no rows') but encoding/json distinguishes them, and a `null` where an agent expects an array is a real footgun (`for x of null` throws in JS; OpenAPI/JSON-Schema generators typed the field as an array). This was not hypothetical: domains/hotam-spec-self/graph.json itself carried `"enforced_by": null` on 2 SETTLED requirements and `"source_refs": null` on 3, which flowed unchanged through `hotam req show --json` into the CLI's machine-readable output before this fix. Every array-typed field across every `--json` command (`due`, `confront`, `inspect`, `req show/list/search/context/related`) now normalizes a nil slice to `[]` at the boundary where it is marshaled — either at construction (`due.go`'s DueReport, list.go's Search) or at the CLI-facing card-building step (query/show.go's requirementToCard/conflictToCard, which is also where a domain's own possibly-null persisted graph.json array field is normalized on its way OUT to an agent, without touching the loader or the persisted format itself). Regression-pinned at the byte level (asserting the literal marshaled JSON text, not just a Go-level len()==0 check) in cmd/hotam/due_test.go, internal/diagnose/confront_test.go, and internal/query/query_test.go. This is a refinement of an existing claim, not a new architectural commitment — no new node was created for it.

requirements.claim: Every Conflict's steward shall be a Stakeholder who is NOT the owner of any of the conflict's members.
requirements.why: A tension lives BETWEEN stakeholders; if the steward owned a side, the tension would be judged by an interested party and quietly resolved in their favor. The structural twin of R-ai-presents-not-decides.

requirements.claim: Two DIFFERENT parties hold two DIFFERENT guarantees over an authored SpecLink, and the boundary between them shall NEVER be blurred: the ENGINE holds the STRUCTURAL floor -- a cited symbol or test really exists, resolves by name against the domain's own authored spec/ tree, is not t.Log-only or otherwise empty (has teeth), is not an unconditional t.Skip, is not formally reused across unrelated requirements, and is not orphaned by a later code change -- while a MIRROR AUDIT, performed by a human or an LLM reading the pair (requirement claim text, authored code+test) without domain-specific heuristics baked into the engine, certifies that the cited test SEMANTICALLY proves the requirement's claim. The engine's structural checks shall NEVER be represented, documented, or relied upon as a substitute for the mirror audit's semantic certification, and the mirror audit's semantic judgment shall NEVER be encoded as a lexical or keyword heuristic inside the graph invariant layer.
requirements.why: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §6 ("Каталог механических проверок") states this boundary as its closing paragraph, calling it out explicitly as an "honesty boundary": "Граница честности: движок держит СТРУКТУРНЫЙ пол. Он не может механически доказать, что тест семантически доказывает требование -- это остаётся зеркальным аудитом." R-authored-spec-links-mechanically-checked (#223) already implements and names the six structural checks (symbol/test resolvable, has-teeth, no-skip, no-unrelated-reuse) and its own WHY paragraph already states the boundary in passing ("HONESTY BOUNDARY (§6): these checks hold the structural floor only... they do NOT and cannot verify that a test SEMANTICALLY proves the requirement's claim; that remains a mirror audit"). This requirement promotes that boundary from a parenthetical inside #223's WHY text to its OWN first-class graph node, because the boundary is a distinct discipline concept with its own failure mode worth naming and citing independently: the session's own history shows repeated near-misses where lexical/keyword heuristics were proposed as a shortcut for semantic judgment (e.g. the CONFRONT opposite-marker gate's own false-positive collisions, R-authored-spec-links-mechanically-checked's own history entry documents one on 2026-07-16) -- the temptation to fold semantic certification into the engine as "just one more check_*" is the exact failure this requirement stands against. PLAN §1 documents the concrete cost of skipping this boundary in the old generator era ("t.Log('no structural atom') in 10 of 22 requirements... a always-green test is not coverage; it creates the appearance of an executable requirement") -- the structural floor prevents THAT specific failure (a hollow always-green test), but does not and cannot prevent a technically-real, technically-passing test that simply tests the wrong thing; only a mirror audit catches that. Naming both halves as one requirement (not two) reflects that the discipline IS the boundary between them, not either half alone.

requirements.claim: Every sub-agent's CLAUDE.md shall contain three parts: scope-filtered embedded methodology thinking, a reference to its parent/senior agent, and its own scope-filtered domain business (CONSTITUTION).
requirements.why: Records the director's explicit framing for the FUTURE shape of a real second agent: a sub-agent inherits (1) base methodology thinking so it can reason like any operator, (2) a connection to its senior/parent agent so it knows where to escalate and whose scope it is bounded within, and (3) its own scope-filtered domain business so it acts only within its delegated concern. Not yet implemented: the per-agent crystal materializer is Planned (not implemented) -- create-agent is a Planned methodology.Tool, and the generator (internal/generator) produces a single consolidated root CLAUDE.md with no per-agent sub-operator files at all. (Historical note: the original scaffolder was tools/create_agent.py, which built domains/<domain>/agents/<name>/ plus tools/gen_spec.py emitting the SCOPE-filtered CONSTITUTION, AGENT-MAP parent linkage, and embedded thinking/tools per agent.) This rule is recorded now, while zero sub-agents are actively spawned, so agent-spawning stays disciplined later -- the triad shape is the contract the future per-agent generator must satisfy when it is implemented. Sibling rule R-claude-md-consolidates-when-single-agent governs WHEN this per-file triad model activates: only once N>1 concurrently-active agents exist; until then the triad content lives merged inside the single root CLAUDE.md (the EMBEDDED-THINKING/CONSTITUTION/OPERATOR-RECURSION blocks). PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists.

requirements.claim: hotam land shall, by default, invoke internal/gate.SelectTier1 for its target anchor and run only the resolved T1 enforcer subset (falling back to the full suite on any selector uncertainty, per R-land-gate-tier-selector-fails-closed's contract) instead of relying solely on all-violations, so each proposal apply pays only the verification cost its own enforced_by tuple demands.
requirements.why: Preserves an intent that was about to be silently lost. R-land-gate-tier-selector and R-land-gate-tier-selector-fails-closed originally claimed this wiring existed (Python-era wording: 'the per-proposal LAND verify step ... shall, by default, run the T1 targeted-enforcer subset'); that was never true in the Go port and both were reworded 2026-07-12 to describe only the standalone hotam gate selector's own contract, dropping the 'wired into land by default' assertion because it wasn't real. But the underlying design intent -- avoid the full-suite time tax on every proposal apply, since most proposals touch exactly one node whose enforced_by already names its enforcer(s) -- is still a live, undecided idea, not a rejected one. Held open here as its own DRAFT rather than quietly dropped when the two ENFORCED claims were narrowed (R-conflict-is-connector-node's generative law: important-yet-invisible tension gets a named node, not silent extinguishment). Scope note for whoever settles this: cmd/hotam/land.go's cmdLand would need to call gate.SelectTier1(targetAnchor, g) after apply and, on Confident=true, run `go test -run '^(name1|name2|...)$' ./...` against the resolved node_ids instead of the current always-full all-violations + implicit full go test at commit boundaries; on Confident=false it must still run the full suite (all-violations already does the structural half; the gap is in NOT also running a scoped `go test` subset). Two open questions for the steward: (1) does 'the full suite' here mean go test ./... or just all-violations (they check different things -- all-violations is structural invariants, go test also runs enforcer test bodies); (2) is a per-land `go test -run` subprocess spawn (Windows subprocess overhead, per the perf note already recorded in R-land-gate-tier-selector's why) actually faster than the current all-violations-only path for the common case, or does it need benchmarking first. Not yet implemented; land's current behavior (apply -> gen-spec -> all-violations, no gate call) is unaffected.

requirements.claim: A task-agent invocation (a sh/Agent-tool call) is a hand -- a one-shot delegated act, not a standing sub-operator.
requirements.why: First half of the split R-task-spawn-is-ephemeral (R-requirement-claim-is-atomic). The user's distinction (hands vs agents): a hand executes one task and reports back, distinct from a domain-delegation sub-operator (R-context-bounded-delegation) which owns a persistent sub-domain. This is a naming/classification discipline -- no check_* can verify 'this call was conceptually a hand', so it stays STRUCTURAL, carried by the spawn-log's own shape (one entry per invocation, no operator-id field implying persistence) rather than a dedicated enforcer.

requirements.claim: A task-agent invocation (a sh/Agent-tool call) is a hand: it returns conclusions and does not persist between invocations.
requirements.why: REJECTED -- REPLACES split into R-task-spawn-is-a-hand + R-task-spawn-no-cross-invocation-persistence (Wave 2 burn-down, decided by ai-agent 2026-07-02) per atomicity discipline (R-requirement-claim-is-atomic). audit_atomicity.py flagged this claim COMPOUND ('and' connects clause with verb, 'and does'): (1) a task-agent invocation IS a hand (a classification claim), and (2) it returns conclusions and does not persist between invocations (a behavioral claim). Splitting separates the naming/classification half from the persistence-behavior half so each can be honestly graded on its own enforceability -- the classification is discipline/naming, the non-persistence half is closer to (but not fully) checkable via the spawn-log's structure. — (was: The user's distinction today: hands vs agents. BUILD-TRIGGER: D3's spawn-log writer exists — the log is the structural recording of this ephemeral act.)

requirements.claim: The spawn_agent tool shall append a spawn-log entry to .runtime/spawn-log.jsonl -- with parent, child kind, task subject, and stamp -- on every invocation.
requirements.why: Not yet implemented: a dedicated spawn-log mechanism. The `spawn_agent` tool is Planned (not Implemented) in methodology.Tools (internal/methodology/tools_data.go), so neither the prompt-composition nor the spawn-log append exists as a hotam CLI command today. (Historical note: the original mechanism was tools/spawn_agent.py, which composed a sub-agent's task prompt by prepending the agent's CLAUDE.md crystal (R-agent-is-recursive-director, R-sub-agent-crystal-triad) and appended a spawn-log entry to spec/.runtime/spawn-log.jsonl, proven by test_spawn_log_written and test_log_only_writes_row_without_composing_prompt.) The 2026-07-10 re-audit's category distinction still holds: 'spawn' here means an explicit spawn_agent invocation composing a domain sub-operator's CLAUDE.md crystal -- there are currently ZERO such sub-operators in this domain (Agent Map: '(no sub-operators yet)'), so zero real invocations is the EXPECTED, honest state, not a broken rule. A host-level Agent-tool spawn (a platform primitive, external to the framework's own code) is category-distinct, covered by R-host-spawn-leaves-trace (hotam-dev). The claim itself stays a single atomic assertion (R-requirement-claim-is-atomic). PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists. The original WHY stands: every explicit spawn must leave a durable runtime trace so the isolation discipline (R-parallel-mutating-agents-use-worktree) is auditable.

requirements.claim: The methodology's Task node type (a modeled work item) and the harness's Action (a fix-the-graph instruction) shall remain distinct types at distinct altitudes — never merged.
requirements.why: SETTLED (P9): the discipline is structural by omission. Hotam-Spec's framework has NO Task type — only the harness Finding (internal/diagnose/finding.go: the typed instruction a what-now Action carries, with Condition/Target/Imperative/Advisory fields). Process.steps carry a forward-compat prose `invokes` field (not a Task type) so the behavioral altitude stays separable from the harness altitude. The two are typed differently by construction: Finding is the harness's typed instruction emitted by diagnose.AllFindings / DiagnoseSignals; any future Task would be a domain-modeled work item under the §Process aspect (internal/ontology/process.go). The altitudes cannot collapse because they live in different namespaces. Implementation: internal/diagnose/finding.go (Finding) + internal/ontology/process.go (Process.steps.invokes) + docs/gen/CONSTITUTION.md.

requirements.claim: The deterministic tension-shortlist tool shall never mutate any domain graph.json: its only outputs are a printed shortlist and an advisory recommendation, and every surfaced pair is a SUSPECT for AI/steward review, never a decided conflict.
requirements.why: The generative-audit tool performs the CONFRONT-adjacent first act of sight, but sight is not decision (R-ai-presents-not-decides). The tension-shortlist capability is realized as `hotam inspect` (internal/diagnose/inspect.go), which is structurally read-only by construction: a Candidate record (internal/diagnose/inspect.go) is ALWAYS advisory â€” it never blocks a write, never changes exit code (exit code always 0 regardless of how many candidates are found), and never decides anything. The recommendation text (advice function) explicitly invokes R-ai-presents-not-decides / R-decided-needs-human-signoff. If the sweep could write a Conflict node it would be deciding a tension exists rather than presenting a candidate; the steward must draft any ProposedConflict from a row. Keeping the tool read-only over the graph preserves the present-never-decide boundary at the generation organ. (Historical note: the original tool was tools/audit_tensions.py; its read-only property was proven by test_audit_never_writes_graph_py.) `hotam inspect` inherits the same read-only discipline by construction â€” it operates on an in-memory graph pointer for read only, with no write path. PROSE (not ENFORCED): the read-only property is upheld by code structure (inspect.go has no graph mutation calls), not by a machine enforcer test that asserts the absence of writes. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestInspect_PresentsOnly_NeverMutatesGraph in internal/diagnose/inspect_test.go mechanically verifies this claim.

requirements.claim: A tool shall emit a deterministic, LLM-free shortlist of SETTLED requirement pairs that might hide an unmediated tension.
requirements.why: The framework held tensions well but historically did not FIND them (0 of 8 conflicts machine-surfaced over its whole history; latent-mining covered 0 pairs; 3 axes never fired). This tool closes the missing first-act-of-sight half of the mediation loop by sweeping the WHOLE settled graph. The capability is realized as `hotam inspect` (internal/diagnose/inspect.go): a deterministic, LLM-free advisory that surfaces tension candidates via four heuristics - InspectSharedAssumptionClusters (pairs sharing a DEAD/HOLDS assumption that one alone cannot break), InspectEntityStateConflicts (two requirements asserting incompatible states on the same entity), InspectLexicalClaimOverlap (natural-language claim pairs with high token overlap but different owners), and InspectAxisCoReference (requirements co-referencing the same Axis with opposing pull). Each surfaced Candidate carries its heuristic name, the member ids, an evidence string, and a score, so a human can review the shortlist. (Historical note: the original tool was tools/audit_tensions.py, which used different heuristics - POLE axis-pole pull / MODAL prohibition-vs-obligation / NOUN cross-owner overlap; the current inspect heuristics reimagines the same first-act-of-sight with graph-structural signals better suited to the current ontology, and those pole/modal/noun tests were not carried forward.) Deterministic and stdlib-only so the shortlist is reproducible and gate-safe (exit code always 0; never gates). PROSE (not ENFORCED): the shortlist is an advisory tool with no enforcer test asserting its heuristics fire on specific fixtures (the inspect heuristics are exercised by table-driven tests in inspect_test.go but as correctness checks, not as enforcement of this requirement). ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestInspect_DeterministicShortlist in internal/diagnose/inspect_test.go mechanically verifies this claim. NOISE FIX 2026-07-13 (external review, P1): on the real hotam-spec-self graph InspectLexicalClaimOverlap was over-firing (1231 of 1232 total candidates), because its stop-word list is generic English closed-class vocabulary and cannot catch a self-describing methodology's own frequently-repeated open-class words (e.g. "requirement" at 12.4% document frequency, "graph" 19.7%, "every" 26.5% across the 234 SETTLED claims) -- two claims sharing only such words were being scored as topically related when they were not. Fixed by computing a document-frequency table over the loaded graph's own SETTLED claims at call time (corpusCommonTokens in internal/diagnose/inspect.go) and excluding any token appearing in more than CorpusCommonTokenFraction (5%) of SETTLED claims from the shared-token overlap count, exactly like a stop word -- this is derived fresh from whatever domain graph is loaded, never a hardcoded word list, so it stays content-free across domains (R-content-free-no-business-data) and adapts automatically to each domain's own vocabulary. Below MinCorpusSizeForFrequencyFilter (8) SETTLED requirements the exclusion is a no-op and behavior is unchanged, since frequency is not a meaningful signal on tiny corpora. Confirmed via total-candidate count on the real graph: 1231 lexical_claim_overlap candidates before -> 234 after (81% reduction), spot-checked to confirm the dropped pairs shared only corpus-common connective words (e.g. "never", "framework", "steward" alone) with no real topical anchor, while the surviving top-scoring candidates now carry genuinely narrow shared vocabulary. `hotam confront` reuses the same exclusion (Confront/confrontHit in internal/diagnose/confront.go), so a candidate sharing only corpus-common words with a SETTLED/REJECTED requirement no longer surfaces as a duplicate/re-litigation suspect. This does NOT guarantee zero false negatives: a real tension whose only shared vocabulary happens to be corpus-common, with no opposite marker and no rarer shared token, is still missed -- the same class of miss the plain stop-word list already accepted for generic English words, now extended to a domain's own frequent words. FALSE-NEGATIVE VALIDATION 2026-07-13 (task #99, independent follow-up review): checked all 8 real steward-confirmed Conflict node member pairs in this graph's own `conflicts` array against InspectLexicalClaimOverlap's output, both with the corpus-frequency filter active and with it disabled (stop-words-only, the pre-fix behavior) via a scratch harness reusing corpusCommonTokens/claimTokens/markerHits directly. Result: 7 of the 8 pairs never fired via lexical overlap even with the filter fully disabled -- they clear neither MinLexicalOverlapTokens nor the owner/marker gate on raw shared-token count alone, consistent with this requirement's own "0 of 8 conflicts machine-surfaced" history note; they were found by the steward through other means entirely, not a filter regression. Exactly ONE pair is a proven corpus-frequency false negative: C-c3911f28 (R-content-free-framework + R-empty-content-is-legitimate) shares 3 tokens ("content" 5.5%, "graph" 19.9%, "spec" 7.6% document frequency) across different owners and clears the firing bar pre-filter, but all three tokens sit over the 5% ceiling and are excluded, dropping shared tokens to 0 post-filter. A threshold sweep showed rescuing it is not worth the cost: raising CorpusCommonTokenFraction to 0.06 alone (letting "content" back in) grows total lexical_claim_overlap candidates from 232 to 316 (+36%); fully rescuing all three tokens needs 0.20, which explodes candidates to 1186, undoing nearly all of the 1231->234 noise fix this note describes above. Accepted as a documented tradeoff rather than tuned: a Conflict node is already graph ground truth (no heuristic needs to "discover" it), and inspect's stated purpose is surfacing NEW undiscovered tension candidates, not reproducing every already-known Conflict. Separately verified the `hotam confront "The system shall enforce every requirement structurally"` hit against R-requirement-freshness-fields flagged by the same review: the shared tokens "system" (2.1% document frequency) and "structurally" (0.8%) are both genuinely rare, well under the 5% ceiling -- this is NOT corpus-filter noise leaking through, it is a real (if weak, score=2, the minimum bar) overlap correctly surfaced by the tool's own stated over-inclusive-by-design philosophy (MinLexicalOverlapTokens's doc comment: cheap false positives preferred over hidden true tensions), left as-is. Both findings pinned by TestInspectLexicalClaimOverlap_KnownConflictGroundTruth (internal/diagnose/inspect_test.go), which loads the real domain graph, checks every real Conflict member pair against InspectLexicalClaimOverlap's live output, and fails loud if a NEW Conflict pair silently joins the miss set beyond the manually-verified allow-list (knownLexicalOverlapMissByDesign) -- the same false-negative risk this requirement's NOISE FIX note already flagged as unverified is now backed by an exact, reproducible count instead of a hypothetical caveat.

requirements.claim: The what_now harness shall surface a CLI-only action on the 'generative-audit' meter when the tension audit has never run or the live SETTLED graph has grown by more than GENERATIVE_AUDIT_STALE_DELTA atoms since the last recorded sweep.
requirements.why: The first act of sight lapses if left to memory as the graph grows. The staleness signal reads the settled_count integer from the last append-only tension-audit.jsonl stamp -- a filesystem read. It is kept CLI-only (like the P6 PENDING_PROPOSAL band), NOT part of diagnose(g): diagnose is rendered by gen_spec into the byte-stable LIVE-STATE (R-deterministic-generation) and is exercised by every graph-generic test on synthetic graphs, so coupling a filesystem read into it would both break determinism and pollute unrelated tests. Surfacing staleness only at the interactive CLI makes 'you have not swept lately' an addressable action without corrupting the pure graph diagnosis.

requirements.claim: The three-cipher pulse (top action / debt / context) shall be structurally present in the LIVE-STATE block of the root CLAUDE.md, regenerated on every gen-spec / land, so the operator's per-turn ORIENT step reads it by reference rather than re-deriving it.
requirements.why: Closes the per-turn edge of the inversion. The three-cipher pulse (top action / debt / context) is required by the boot ritual to be cited in the first sentence of every substantive reply (R-boot-cite-in-first-sentence). The pulse IS structurally generated: BuildLiveState (internal/generator/livestate.go) reads diagnose.DiagnoseSignals for the top-action line, computes the SETTLED/DRAFT/OPEN/closeable-debt counts for the debt line, and emits the context line (ctxLineStatic — the honest UNMEASURED explanation) verbatim; this runs on every `hotam gen-spec` and every `hotam land` (cmd/hotam/land.go step b → genSpec → BuildLiveState). The Mediation loop step 1 (ORIENT, embedded in CLAUDE.md) instructs the operator to reload this pulse by reference. NOT YET IMPLEMENTED is the automatic per-turn HOOK INJECTION: emit-cipher is Planned (not Implemented) in methodology.Tools, so the pulse is present in LIVE-STATE but the operator reads it manually per the ORIENT step, not via an auto-injected hook. (Historical note: the original emit_cipher.py was a UserPromptSubmit hook in .claude/settings.local.json that read the LIVE-STATE sentinels and emitted a JSON hookSpecificOutput consumed by the host harness, structurally forcing the pulse into every user turn; proven by test_user_prompt_submit_hook_emits_cipher.) The original WHY stands: structural presence of the pulse prevents the operator from forgetting to orient. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer test exists. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestBuildLiveState_ThreeCipherPulsePresent in internal/generator/atoms_livestate_test.go mechanically verifies this claim against the generated crystal on the fixture graph.

requirements.claim: Every ticket shall carry an append-only ## History section in which each mutation (create, status move, comment, text change) records one machine-recognisable entry, with a text change snapshotting the prior text.
requirements.why: Not yet implemented: the ticket engine and its History-append mechanism. All ticket-* tools (ticket_create, ticket_move, ticket_comment, ticket_edit) are Planned (not Implemented) in methodology.Tools (internal/methodology/tools_data.go), so neither the on-disk ticket format nor the History-append mechanism exists as a hotam CLI command today. (Historical note: the original mechanism was the ticket_* tool family — tools/ticket_create.py, ticket_move.py, ticket_comment.py, ticket_edit.py — where every ticket_* mutator appended exactly one History line, and ticket_edit snapshotted the OLD title/body into that line so the edit trail survived the edit; proven by tests/test_tool_ticket_create.py, test_tool_ticket_move.py, test_tool_ticket_comment.py, test_tool_ticket_edit.py.) The steward's original intent still stands: status history AND text-change history should be kept automatically so a ticket's audit trail is durable across mutations. When the ticket engine is implemented, each mutator (create/move/comment/edit) must append exactly one History entry, with ticket_edit snapshotting the prior title/body. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists.

requirements.why: The encapsulation the steward demanded: creation, status movement and auto-history must be owned by the tools so the History trail is never skipped. Its enforceable teeth live in R-ticket-carries-history (each mutation demonstrably appends History); this atom names the discipline that keeps hands off the header.

requirements.claim: The full go test ./... suite shall remain the mandatory verification gate at wave and commit boundaries -- the T1 targeted-enforcer tier (hotam gate / internal/gate.SelectTier1) is a standalone advisory selection tool, never a substitute for the full-suite run a steward or wave-closing agent performs before committing.
requirements.why: Tiering exists to relieve the redundant per-proposal tax, not to weaken the wave/commit-boundary guarantee that the WHOLE graph is still structurally sound after a batch of changes -- a targeted T1 pass on proposal N does not prove proposal N did not regress something proposal N-3 touched. This is inherently a discipline/procedural claim about WHEN each tier is invoked (the Mediation loop step 6 LAND names `go test ./...` as MANDATORY at wave/commit boundaries, vs. the standalone `hotam gate <anchor>` command for per-node T1 selection) rather than a graph-checkable structural property, so it is marked STRUCTURAL / INHERENTLY_PROSE rather than claiming a machine enforcer that cannot honestly exist for a human-involved boundary. CONSISTENT WITH THE GATE-SELECTOR REWORK: the prior claim asserted the T1 tier ran 'by default' inside the LAND step; that automatic wiring does not exist. `hotam land` (cmd/hotam/land.go) runs apply -> gen-spec -> all-violations and does NOT call gate.SelectTier1 at all; the T1 selector is reachable only via the standalone `hotam gate <anchor>` command (cmd/hotam/gate_cmd.go). (Historical note: the original wiring was apply_proposal.py consulting tools/gate.py, invoking pytest; that path was not carried forward.) The lost intent -- wiring T1 selection as LAND's default verify tier -- is preserved as its own open item, not silently dropped: see R-t1-selector-as-land-default (DRAFT) and the reworded R-land-gate-tier-selector / R-land-gate-tier-selector-fails-closed (both narrowed to the standalone selector's own contract). The claim's '--' scope-disclaimer convention (kept from the Wave 8 atomicity pass) marks the clause after '--' as a scope/boundary clause of the SAME rule (defining exactly how far the T1 tier is allowed to reach), not a second independent obligation. No semantic change to the promise.

requirements.claim: Every tool registered in the methodology.Tools registry whose entry opens with a Canon: §<topic> marker shall be projected into a STRUCTURAL tool-derived requirement R-tool-<basename> with that tool's claim text, enforced by its named enforcer test when one exists.
requirements.why: The sensor-substrate inversion taken one step deeper: not only does code generate the operator's prompt, the tool IS its own requirement. The claim is the tool's purpose, the registry entry is the declaration, the enforcer test (when present) is the guard. Removing the tool removes the R; lying in the purpose is caught by the enforcer test failing (when one exists). This eliminates the prose gap between 'R written in graph' and 'tool implementing R'. The projection is realized by internal/generator/tool_reqs.go: a static data table (toolRequirementData) carries one ToolRequirement per tool (Basename, CanonSection, Claim, Enforcer); ScanToolRequirements() projects each into R-tool-<basename> (underscores→hyphens); BuildToolDerivedSection() renders them into FRAMEWORK-INVARIANTS.md under 'Tool-derived requirements', and BuildFrameworkInvariants also lists them in the constitution index. (Historical note: the original mechanism scanned spec/tools/*.py for module docstrings opening with 'Canon: §<topic> — <claim>'; that filesystem scan was replaced by the explicit data table, since tools are now CLI subcommands in cmd/hotam/, not discoverable .py modules with parseable docstrings — but the 1:1 tool→R projection discipline is preserved.) PROSE (not ENFORCED): the projection itself has no enforcer test (proven historically by test_tool_derived_requirements.py); the data table is maintained by hand alongside the methodology.Tools registry. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestScanToolRequirements_ProjectsEveryEntryToRequirement in internal/generator/tool_reqs_test.go mechanically verifies this claim.

requirements.claim: Delegation of the steward's personal-signature duty to an agent shall be valid ONLY when granted EXPLICITLY -- per-case or for a declared campaign in advance -- never implied or standing by default.
requirements.why: M5, REPLACES split into R-trust-anchor-mechanism + R-trust-anchor-delegation-explicit-only per atomicity discipline (R-requirement-claim-is-atomic). Steward verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «человек обязан сам подписать, если только явно не делегирует это в каждом случае или на кампанию вперед» (English: 'the human is obliged to sign personally, unless he explicitly delegates it, either per-case or for a campaign in advance'). This atom lands the EXCEPTION half: delegation is not the default and is not implicit from e.g. an agent being given repo write access -- it requires an explicit steward grant, scoped either to one case or to a named campaign declared in advance. RETIRED MECHANISM (steward triage decision B1, 2026-07-09, verbatim: «снести старую jsonl-систему делегаций полностью»): the original mechanism -- domains/hotam-spec-self/delegations.jsonl, a durable committed append-only JSONL ledger written by tools/record_delegation.py, cross-checked against Conflict.lifecycle markers by tests/test_delegation_marker_honesty.py -- has been torn down completely. The seed record DEL-1 is preserved verbatim as inert historical record in docs/development/DELEGATIONS-HISTORY.md and in the Conflict's own Signoff.instrument="DEL-1" field (R-signoff-preserved-in-substrate), no longer mechanically cross-checked. NOT YET IMPLEMENTED: this claim is to be upheld by the file-based mechanism R-delegation-is-a-file established for task hand-offs -- a delegation is a versioned delegations/DG-<n>.md file, created and closed only via a delegate command, with git history as the audit trail. Neither the delegate tool nor the delegations/ file format exists: `delegate` is not registered in methodology.Tools at all (not even Planned), and no delegations/ directory exists. The steward's decision principle still stands: delegation requires explicit, scoped, advance grant -- never implied from write access. When the delegate tool is implemented, the file-based mechanism (delegations/DG-<n>.md, created/closed via a hotam delegate command) should implement this. RECLASSIFIED 2026-07-13: enforceability ENFORCEABLE -> INHERENTLY_PROSE (agent consultation, fm) -- the normative condition ("valid ONLY when granted EXPLICITLY, never implied") governs a human act (the steward's decision to delegate); a tool can record that a grant was made, but cannot structurally detect the ABSENCE of an implied delegation happening outside its own records. The mechanical remainder -- that a delegation grant, once made, is captured as a DG-file -- is the separate, genuinely enforceable concern of R-delegation-is-a-file (which stays ENFORCEABLE, pending the delegate tool).

requirements.claim: Every decision shall be personally signed by the human steward -- today: a decided_by: Stakeholder.id field on the DECIDED Conflict plus git commit authorship.
requirements.why: M5, REPLACES split into R-trust-anchor-mechanism + R-trust-anchor-delegation-explicit-only per atomicity discipline (R-requirement-claim-is-atomic). Steward verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «человек обязан сам подписать, если только явно не делегирует это в каждом случае или на кампанию вперед» (English: 'the human is obliged to sign personally, unless he explicitly delegates it, either per-case or for a campaign in advance'). This atom lands the DEFAULT (personal signature) half: the steward's answer is that the existing decided_by + commit-author pairing IS the anchor; no additional cryptographic layer is required today. The mechanism is Conflict.DecidedBy (internal/ontology/conflict.go:48, a Stakeholder.id reference on every DECIDED Conflict) plus git commit authorship (the repo's own author trail). Cadence is 'per decision' (every DECIDED Conflict), not periodic -- the steward signs each contradiction resolution as it happens, which is stronger than 'quarterly/per-PR' polling. ENFORCED by two atomic checks registered directly in the invariant registry (internal/invariants/conflict_decided_held.go): check_decided_has_nonempty_decided_by (asserts DecidedBy is non-empty on every DECIDED Conflict) and check_decided_by_is_known_stakeholder (asserts the DecidedBy value is a registered Stakeholder.id), both exercised by conflict_decided_held_test.go. (Historical note: enforced_by originally cited a thin non-registered delegator wrapping sub-checks in hotam_spec/invariants.py ~line 3415; the two current atomic checks ARE real ALL registry members, so the bijection holds without the delegator indirection.)

requirements.claim: The what_now harness shall surface every UNCERTAIN assumption carrying at least UNCERTAIN_AGING_MIN_DEPENDENTS dependent requirements as one P4 OPEN_ITEM action asking the steward to resolve the doubt.
requirements.why: Blind spot found in the fxx assumption-life audit (2026-07-03): DEAD lights up P2 DRIFT_FALLOUT and HOLDS is calm, but an UNCERTAIN assumption aged invisibly — nowhere in the harness — even though the three real UNCERTAIN assumptions carry 58 / 37 / 9 dependent requirements (A-bootstrap-self-applies is the single largest silent question in the graph). uncertain_assumptions(g) is a pure status filter (peer of dead_assumptions); the K=UNCERTAIN_AGING_MIN_DEPENDENTS=5 threshold (chosen against the real graph so all three high-fan-out questions clear it and low-fan-out doubt stays quiet) gates one P4 action per aged assumption, pointing the steward at the AssumptionTransition kill-path (DEAD) or re-affirmation (HOLDS). Graph-only and deterministic, so it lives in what_now.diagnose(g) exactly like the P2 DEAD-fallout band, not as a CLI-only filesystem band.

requirements.claim: Detection of 'uncrystallizable knowledge = missing type' is human judgment: the operator records the candidate in the graph as an OPEN requirement (or a DRAFT), and the steward decides whether to add the ontology type.
requirements.why: M30. DECIDED 2026-06-30: human judgment, not automated. Rationale: R-uncrystallizable-is-missing-type (SETTLED P6) already establishes that the operator records the signal as a node; the §Conscience property-sweep surfaces the meta-signal structurally when a class of contradictions cannot be expressed by existing critical-core invariants. Automating the type-creation decision would violate R-ai-presents-not-decides. The whole audit-backlog-residue checkpoint pattern + the DRAFT queue IS the recording mechanism. The steward is the decider; the graph is the recorder. The §Conscience concept exists as methodology prose (internal/methodology/sections_data.go: §Conscience section, noting 'go test ./internal/invariants/...' as the conceptual sweep over the critical-core invariants named in internal/generator/constitution.go criticalCoreNames). NOT YET IMPLEMENTED: a property-test sweep over the critical core to flag a class of contradictions no existing invariant can express (the §Conscience concept-map entry reflects this: 0 checks, tests counted from static data, no mapped definition). (Historical note: the original test_conscience.py was a Hypothesis-based property test over CRITICAL_CORE_INVARIANTS; it was not carried forward.) The human-judgment core of this claim is unchanged and inherently unautomatable: the operator records the signal as a graph node, the steward decides. STRUCTURAL / INHERENTLY_PROSE: the claim's own nature is a human-judgment decision boundary that no machine check_* can honestly enforce (R-enforceability-kind-declared).
